# Codex TB-13 Ship Audit — implementation-paranoid

**Role**: skeptical adversarial implementer-reviewer for the TB-13 (CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx) ship-gate dual external audit. Independent of Gemini ship audit (parallel, architectural angle).

**Mandate**: Class 3 (money/collateral surface — first agent-signed economic mutator that locks Coin into conditional collateral and issues YES/NO claims). Per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`: VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS. Round cap = 2 per `feedback_elon_mode_policy`.

## Audit target

- Charter: `handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md`
- Architect ruling lossless: `handover/directives/2026-05-03_TB13_TO_TB17_POST_TB12_ARCHITECT_RULING.md`
- Recursive self-audit: `handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md`
- Real-LLM regression smoke: `handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/README.md`

```text
TB-12 ship range:  fa36eca           (TB-12 baseline 759/0/150)
TB-13 range:       fa36eca..HEAD     (this audit)
HEAD: cargo test --workspace = 783 passed / 0 failed / 150 ignored (+24 net)
```

Net-new for TB-13 (commits to anchor your reads):

- `32aab27` Atom 0 + 0.5 — Charter + legacy CPMM forward-fence + label
- `70303af` Atom 1 — typed_tx schemas (3 variants + 4 newtypes + 3 SigningPayloads + 8 unit tests)
- `1806432` Atoms 2+3+5 — Sequencer dispatch + conservation invariant + SG-13.x integration tests
- `17d4a3b` Atom 6(a) — Recursive self-audit
- `7aac629` Atom 6(a.5) — Real-LLM regression smoke

## The 9 mandated audit questions (per architect Part A §4 + charter §3 Atom 6.b)

**Q1 (anti-mint)**: Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetMint accept arm. The contract: `balances_t[owner]` debit BY exactly `amount`; `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` credit BY exactly `amount`; equal YES + NO shares minted (claims, NOT Coin). Σ holdings (6-holding sum incl. conditional_collateral_t) bit-equal pre/post.

**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Can Redeem fire without a system-emitted resolution? Trace dispatch arm. Sequencer must validate `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}`; Open / Expired → `RedeemBeforeResolution`. Test: `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` covers both Open and Expired.

**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.

**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.

**Q5 (complete-set balanced)**: Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` hold after every transition? The invariant: for every event in conditional_collateral_t, `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`. The MIN form (vs strict `Σ_yes==Σ_no==collateral`) was discovered mid-development as the correct form post-redemption — losing-side shares strand above collateral. Trace the invariant body in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` and challenge the rule. Tests: `sg_13_1` (post-mint balanced; both equal), `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` (post-redeem MIN form).

**Q6 (seed solvency)**: Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? Trace MarketSeed accept arm: `collateral_amount > 0` else `InsufficientCollateral` (SG-13.4); `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount` else `InsufficientBalanceForMint` (SG-13.3). Test: `sg_13_3`, `sg_13_4`, `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected`.

**Q7 (shares-not-Coin)**: Are conditional shares anywhere counted as Coin? `total_supply_micro` must EXCLUDE `conditional_share_balances_t`. Read the function body and confirm. Architect CR-13.3 + SG-13.2 explicit.

**Q8 (underflow)**: Could a malformed `ShareAmount.units: u128` underflow at redeem? Sequencer must check `owned_units >= share_amount.units` (RedeemMoreThanOwned) AND `event_collateral.micro_units() as u128 >= share_amount.units` (InsufficientCollateral) BEFORE the subtraction. Trace both gates; verify the type-cast `event_collateral.micro_units() as u128` is sound (i64 → u128 widens; negative collateral would be caught by 6-holding sum but is structurally impossible).

**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Does any new TB-13 module file import legacy `prediction_market`? Trace `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` span detector. The fence's authoring-marker rule: only spans whose first non-blank line matches `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13` / `// TB-13 ` / `//! TB-13 ` / `/// TB-13 ` are in scope. Verify the rule cannot be bypassed by writing a TB-13 import OUTSIDE a TB-13-marker span (e.g., adding a `use crate::prediction_market::BinaryMarket` at the top of `src/state/sequencer.rs` without a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be caught). Is the fence robust enough?

## Specifically scrutinize (RQ1-RQ7)

**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? Trace `src/state/sequencer.rs` CompleteSetRedeem step 1.

**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
- balances_t row decreases by `amount`.
- conditional_collateral_t row increases by `amount`.
- conditional_share_balances_t both YES + NO sides increase by `amount.units` (claims, not Coin; not in supply sum).
- 6 holdings sum: pre = post. PASS?

At CompleteSetRedeem (winning side, e.g., Yes after Finalized):
- conditional_collateral_t row decreases by `share_amount.units`.
- conditional_share_balances_t YES side decreases by `share_amount.units`.
- balances_t row increases by `share_amount.units`.
- conditional_share_balances_t NO side UNCHANGED (stranded losing share).
- 6 holdings sum: pre = post. PASS?

At MarketSeed:
- balances_t row decreases by `collateral_amount`.
- conditional_collateral_t row increases by `collateral_amount`.
- conditional_share_balances_t both YES + NO sides increase by `collateral_amount.units` for the provider.
- 6 holdings sum: pre = post. PASS?

**RQ3 — Replay determinism**: Two replays of the same chain must produce byte-identical conditional_collateral_t + conditional_share_balances_t. Verify via the smoke evidence at `handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/single_n1_mathd_algebra_171/replay_report.json`: `economic_state_reconstructed: true`. Also verify the round-trip at `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs::sg_13_1` post-mint state.

**RQ4 — Re-mint after partial redeem (adversarial)**: Trace this sequence:
1. Mint K Coin → YES=K, NO=K, coll=K.
2. State flips Finalized.
3. Redeem M from YES → YES=K-M, NO=K, coll=K-M, balance += M.
4. Mint J more Coin → YES=K-M+J, NO=K+J, coll=K-M+J.
5. Redeem J from YES → YES=K-M, NO=K+J, coll=K-M, balance += J.
Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` (MIN form) hold throughout? Does any sequence break the 6-holding sum? Is there an adversarial pattern where a single agent profits unfairly?

**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

**RQ6 — Forward-fence span detector robustness**: The fence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` only checks lines inside spans with a TB-13 authoring marker. A new TB-13 file that does NOT carry a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be in scope. Is this exploitable? Recommend: should the fence also scan for TB-13-named files (`src/state/conditional_market.rs` etc.) regardless of marker, OR is the marker-discipline sufficient?

**RQ7 — STEP_B protocol compliance**: TB-13 modifies `src/state/sequencer.rs` (restricted file per CLAUDE.md Code Standard). The TB-12 precedent did so via direct edit (additive dispatch arms). TB-13 follows the same pattern. Is this within STEP_B? Or should TB-13 have used parallel-branch A/B?

## Verdict format

End your audit with one of:

```text
## VERDICT: PASS
(All Q1-Q9 + RQ1-RQ7 cleared; ship is clean.)
```

```text
## VERDICT: CHALLENGE
- <claim id> CHALLENGE: <one-line reason + line refs>
- <repeat per challenge>
(round-2 will trigger feedback_elon_mode_policy auto-execute on
determinate-best surgical patch.)
```

```text
## VERDICT: VETO
- <claim id> VETO: <one-line BLOCKING reason + line refs>
(VETO blocks ship per feedback_dual_audit_conflict; VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS.)
```

Include conviction (low/medium/high) + recommendation (PROCEED to SHIP / FIX-THEN-PROCEED / REDESIGN).

Cite file:line for every finding.

Save your audit to: handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.

Recommend: jsonl as primary ground-truth source, WAL as structural cross-reference, stderr as fallback for fine-grained diagnostics. OR (more invasive): emit OmegaInvoke/Accepted/Rejected/Error to ledger from `oracle.verify_omega_detailed` call sites (touches evaluator.rs but not bus.rs/kernel.rs — outside STEP_B_PROTOCOL restricted-file zone).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8157:handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:730:**Protocol for restricted-file changes**: `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` (necessity audit → parallel branch → A/B statistical test → merge on empirical win only). Do NOT directly edit restricted files even with authorization; always A/B test.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8187:handover/ai-direct/EXT_AUDIT_PHASE_8A/brief.md:4:**Change scope**: `src/bus.rs` — restricted file (requires dual external audit per STEP_B_PROTOCOL).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8229:handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:197:Node-count distribution per solve (addressing P2-12): to be extracted from gp_payload when Appendix C is finalized; preliminary check shows some B solves terminate in 1 `step` node (multi-line inline proof), others in multi-node chains. The solve-count claim in § 4.1 is robust to this distinction; the "multi-agent collaboration" interpretation is restricted to multi-node chains and is explicitly scoped in § 7 Limitation 5.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8329:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:1:# Step-B Protocol: Restricted-File Changes via Parallel Branch Experiment
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8330:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3:**Scope**: any change to files in CLAUDE.md's restricted list (currently `kernel.rs`, `bus.rs`, `wallet.rs`). Also applicable to any proposal that touches "institution" per C-031.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8331:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:13:- Is a less-invasive alternative available? (same effect, no restricted-file touch)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8332:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:17:**Gate**: if both auditors say "less-invasive alternative exists", take that path instead. If both say "change is necessary", proceed to Phase 1. If they disagree, take the conservative verdict per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8333:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:59:- **Sample size justification**: binomial approximation — a 3-solve difference (6 pp on N=50) is ~2σ above null; 6-solve difference (12 pp) is ~4σ. Pre-register **strict-win threshold = ΔSolveRate ≥ 3** (consistent with v3.1 decision rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8334:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:62:  - `ΔSolveRate ≥ 3`: treatment strict win → merge candidate
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8335:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:107:1. **C-010**: external audit at 3 junctures (necessity, diff, verdict). Researcher cannot self-approve a restricted change.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8336:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:115:- **Statistical power** is limited by frozen N=50. Small effects (Δ = 1-2 solves) stay inconclusive. Pre-register a larger N (100? 244?) if effect is expected small.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8356:handover/ai-direct/PHASE_Z_PRIME_STRICT_ALIGNMENT_PLAN_2026-04-22.md:106:  - **Agent F (Codex)** if element touches bus/kernel (restricted)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8357:handover/ai-direct/PHASE_Z_PRIME_STRICT_ALIGNMENT_PLAN_2026-04-22.md:150:| Agent B / F | Codex | code-scan for candidate mappings, restricted-file touches | 1, 3 |

STEP_B_PROTOCOL applies if any actual behavior change to those files becomes necessary.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9297:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:45:C_i is NOT restricted to the golden-path payload. Failed-branch tokens MUST be counted. Goal: a system that wastes 1M tokens to find a 1k-token proof has lower PPUT than one that finds the same proof in 100k tokens.

handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:27:-   **Evidence**: The `total_supply_micro` function in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152-172` enumerates exactly four coin-holding sub-indexes (balances, escrows, stakes, challenge_cases). It does **not** iterate over `economic_state_t.node_positions_t`. This is the definitive guard against `NodePosition.amount` being counted as part of the monetary base.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:29:-   **Evidence**: The schema definition at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:658-660` explicitly documents `NodePosition` as "NOT a Coin holding" and "NOT in total_supply_micro", aligning documentation with implementation.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:67:**6. Does the flat NodePositionsIndex extend cleanly to TB-13 CompleteSet (which will introduce real YES/NO claims) without schema collision?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:69:**PASS.** The separation of concerns is clean. `NodePosition` serves as a historical record of initial risk-taking, while TB-13's `CompleteSet` will introduce a new set of objects representing tradable claims. There is no schema collision. A `MarketSeedTx` (TB-13) can reference a `node_id` (a `WorkTx` ID), and the `NodePosition` index provides the on-chain context for why a market on that node exists, but the two data structures do not overlap or conflict. This is a sound substrate.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:84:-   **NodePosition counted as Coin**: Impossible. See Q4. The `total_supply_micro` function (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152`) is the sole authority and does not include `node_positions_t`.
handover/audits/run_gemini_co1_7_5_round1_audit.py:59:**Q4. handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:89:- The spec says STEP_B comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" rather than code similarity at `handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:681` and `handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:748-755`.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:91:- Gemini's prior cross-review named differential fuzzing as the strongest STEP_B test at `handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:51-58`. v1.1 still lists test names, not a runner ABI or fixture format, at `handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:654-681`.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:97:- Add a STEP_B conformance section with:
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:103:- Blocks CO P1? Yes for STEP_B atoms CO1.1.4/CO1.1.5. Without this, "spec conformance" is a human review label, not a gate.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:135:- CO1.7 implements `TransitionTx` with `i64`, while CO1.1.4 keeps bus/kernel APIs in `f64`. A later P2.0a migration changes serialization and hashes for already-written transition records, breaking replay and STEP_B comparison.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:138:- Move `MicroCoin(i64)`, `StakeMicroCoin`, and `RoyaltyRatePpm` to a P1 prerequisite atom before CO1.7 and before any STEP_B branch serializes tx records.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:146:STEP_B-ready: NO.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:156:- CO1.1.4 starts after this freeze; one branch preserves legacy env/f64/invest behavior as compatibility code, another removes it as out-of-scope. Both can claim compliance because the spec lacks transition coverage and STEP_B byte fixtures.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:159:- Patch `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC` to v1.2 or v1.1a with the transition coverage matrix, hidden-input table expansion, STEP_B runner/serialization rules, concurrency sequencer, MicroCoin prerequisite, rounding rule, stake return, task-expiry refund, finalize batch order, and challenge-window edge rule.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2532:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2561:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2563:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2953:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3134:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3187:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3236:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3247:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3298:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:45:## Q4 Combined STEP_B argument rebase
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:48:-   **Functional-coupling criterion**: This is a much stronger and more objective criterion than the original "minimum sufficient version" argument, which `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` correctly frames as advisory. Functional coupling—where each half of a change is a compile-error or a no-op without the other—provides a rigorous, machine-checkable justification for treating the changes as a single atomic unit. This strengthens the integrity of the STEP_B process.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:50:-   **Generalizability**: This justification is not a one-off. The principle of functional coupling can and should be used as the standard for justifying future combined STEP_B ceremonies. It provides a clear test: if the changes can be landed and tested independently without being dead code, they should be separate ceremonies. If not, they can be combined.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:72:-   **D3 compile-coherence**: The STEP_B ceremony itself serves as the test for the compile-time coherence of the D3 wiring graph. No additional test is required for this part.
handover/audits/STEP_B_PREFLIGHT_TB9_ATOM1_2026-05-02.md:1:# STEP_B Preflight — TB-9 Atom 1: Durable Agent Keystore
handover/audits/STEP_B_PREFLIGHT_TB9_ATOM1_2026-05-02.md:7:## §1 STEP_B classification per `feedback_step_b_protocol`
handover/audits/STEP_B_PREFLIGHT_TB9_ATOM1_2026-05-02.md:9:`agent_keypairs.rs` is the agent-side equivalent of `system_keypair.rs`; under the standard restricted-file list (`bus.rs / kernel.rs / wallet.rs` + auth primitives by extension), it triggers STEP_B. Per the protocol: parallel-branch A/B with side-by-side test on existing precedent.
handover/audits/STEP_B_PREFLIGHT_TB9_ATOM1_2026-05-02.md:72:## §6 STEP_B verdict
handover/audits/run_gemini_co1_7_extra_round3_audit.py:38:- **MF4**: Sequencer placement migrated from Kernel to TuringBus. Kernel UNTOUCHED. STEP_B becomes single-file ceremony on bus.rs. Cleaner layering — Kernel preserves "pure topology" doctrine; runtime drivers (Sequencer + future) live at TuringBus level.
handover/audits/run_gemini_co1_7_extra_round3_audit.py:121:    ("XREF: STEP_B_PROTOCOL", "handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md"),
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:21:**Q5. STEP_B atom risk:** The designation of `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` splits as parallel branches under the STEP_B protocol is a sophisticated and appropriate response to a high-risk refactoring task. While a sequential refactor might seem "safer," it would be significantly slower and forfeit the immense value of a second, independent implementation from Codex. The parallel approach, governed by the Tri-Model Protocol's cross-comparison and tie-breaker mechanism, is a superior strategy for mitigating risk in these architecturally critical atoms. It trades simple safety for robust, verified correctness. **[PASS]**

handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:1:# TB-13 Recursive Self-Audit — CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:22:| Art. 0.2 Tape Canonical                  | 3 NEW typed-tx variants (CompleteSetMint / CompleteSetRedeem / MarketSeed) canonical-encoded. 3 NEW state-root domain prefixes (`COMPLETE_SET_MINT_DOMAIN_V1` / `COMPLETE_SET_REDEEM_DOMAIN_V1` / `MARKET_SEED_DOMAIN_V1`). Replay-deterministic from typed-tx fields. | `tb_13_complete_set_mint_round_trips_canonical` + `tb_13_complete_set_redeem_round_trips_canonical` + `tb_13_market_seed_round_trips_canonical` + `tb_13_signing_payloads_deterministic_digest` |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:23:| Art. I.1 5-step compile loop closure     | Conditional collateral & share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome (CR-13.6: "Price / share state cannot override predicates or challenge outcome"). | TB-13 dispatch arms operate purely on `economic_state_t.{balances_t, conditional_collateral_t, conditional_share_balances_t}`; predicate-evaluation paths (TB-3 Work / TB-4 Verify+Challenge / TB-5 ChallengeResolve / TB-8 FinalizeReward) UNCHANGED. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:25:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:26:| Art. V.1.3 Anti-Oreo                     | All 3 TB-13 typed-tx are AGENT-SIGNED (CompleteSetMint / CompleteSetRedeem / MarketSeed). NO new system_tx variant introduced. Provider funds are EXPLICIT in MarketSeedTx (NO automatic seed; CR-13.1 + CR-13.2 forbid ghost / automatic liquidity). | `submit_agent_tx` ingress agent-fall-through arm extended for 3 new variants (verified via reading `src/state/sequencer.rs:1996..2002`). NO `emit_system_tx` arm added for TB-13. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:36:| Q-projection determinism                  | Conditional collateral is a deterministic projection of accepted CompleteSetMint / MarketSeed tx fields (`amount.micro_units()` / `collateral_amount.micro_units()`). Conditional share balances are deterministic projections (Yes + No each = `amount.micro_units() as u128`). Redeem deterministically debits winning side + collateral by `share_amount.units`. No environmental input. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:37:| Cross-instance replay equality            | TB-3..TB-12 replay invariants unchanged. TB-13 dispatch arms add to `q_next.economic_state_t.{balances_t, conditional_collateral_t, conditional_share_balances_t}` only — each mutation is field-by-field deterministic. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:48:| **6-holding CTF (post-TB-13)**            | Σ holdings = balances_t + escrows_t + stakes_t + challenge_cases_t + conditional_collateral_t. Atom 3 extends the TB-7R 5-holding sum to 6 by adding `conditional_collateral_t` per architect CR-13.4 ("Locked collateral is Coin holding"). conditional_share_balances_t INTENTIONALLY OMITTED per CR-13.3 + SG-13.2. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:49:| `assert_total_ctf_conserved` post-mint    | `sg_13_1_mint_one_coin_yields_one_yes_plus_one_no_total_coin_conserved` + `sg_13_2_yes_no_shares_not_in_total_coin_supply` + `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`: balance debit = collateral credit, total preserved bit-for-bit. PASS.  |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:50:| `assert_total_ctf_conserved` post-redeem  | Redeem: collateral debit + balance credit (1:1). `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` exercises mint+redeem and asserts `assert_total_ctf_conserved(pre, post, &[]).is_ok()`. PASS. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:51:| `assert_total_ctf_conserved` post-seed    | `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`: provider balance debit = collateral credit. CTF preserved. PASS. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:53:| `assert_complete_set_balanced` (NEW)      | For every event in conditional_collateral_t: `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`. Pre-resolution (mint+seed): Σ_yes == Σ_no == collateral (both equal trivially equivalent). Post-redeem: winning side equals collateral; losing side may be larger (stranded zero-value claims). Verified: `sg_13_1` post-mint balanced; `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`; `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` post-redeem balanced. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:55:**MIN-semantics rationale**: a strict `Σ_yes == collateral AND Σ_no == collateral` requirement does NOT hold post-redemption (the losing side has stranded shares above the now-decremented collateral). MIN form correctly captures: every Coin in collateral can be redeemed by the winning side, and no winning-side share is unbacked. This was discovered mid-test by my own halting-trigger guard `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`; the strict-equality form initially failed and was replaced with MIN form. The bug-find demonstrates the recursive self-audit harness working as intended.
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:65:| 4.1 Redeem requires resolved task_market state | `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Open, Expired}` → `RedeemBeforeResolution`. Verified: `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` covers both Open and Expired. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:66:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:67:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:68:| 4.4 Owner share-balance gate               | `RedeemMoreThanOwned` if `conditional_share_balances_t[owner][event_id].{yes|no}` < `share_amount.units`. Verified: `halt_redeem_more_than_owned_rejected`. |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:69:| 4.5 Collateral coverage gate               | `InsufficientCollateral` if `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` < `share_amount.units` (defensive; should never fire if `assert_complete_set_balanced` holds). |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:93:| total_supply_micro mutation incorrect (CTF conservation failure) | NOT triggered. `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` + `halt_shares_not_counted_as_coin` PASS. |

handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:95:| MarketSeedTx succeeds without balance debit (architect SG-13.3)   | NOT triggered. `halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected` + `sg_13_3_market_seed_fails_if_provider_lacks_balance` PASS. |

handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1:# Gemini TB-13 Ship Audit — CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx (Class 3)
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:26:**1. Does CompleteSetMint create or destroy money? (Must be balance↔collateral migration only.)**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:28:*   **Verification**: The `CompleteSetMintTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1904-1949` performs two primary economic mutations:
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:30:    2.  `*collateral_entry = ...` credits `q_next.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t` by the same `mint.amount`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:31:*   The conservation invariant function `assert_total_ctf_conserved` in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:159-179` is extended to include `conditional_collateral_t` in its sum of holdings.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:32:*   **Finding**: The transaction is a pure migration of value from one Coin-holding ledger (`balances_t`) to another (`conditional_collateral_t`). The total supply of Coin is bit-for-bit conserved. The subsequent minting of `conditional_share_balances_t` does not affect Coin supply, as shares are correctly excluded from the `total_supply_micro` sum (see Q7).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:35:**2. Can Redeem fire without a system-emitted resolution? (Must be sequencer-rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`.)**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:37:*   **Verification**: The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964-1983` performs a state lookup on `task_markets_t` for the given `event_id`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:38:*   The `match` statement explicitly handles `TaskMarketState::Open` and `TaskMarketState::Expired` by returning `Err(TransitionError::RedeemBeforeResolution)`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:42:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`.)**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:44:*   **Verification**: The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964-1983` contains a `match` on the tuple `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:*   Any other combination on a resolved market, such as `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)`, falls through to the wildcard arm which returns `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:50:**4. Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:53:    *   `CompleteSetMintTx` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1904-1949`): `balances_t` debit is equal to `conditional_collateral_t` credit. Both are in the 6-holding sum. Net change is zero.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:54:    *   `CompleteSetRedeemTx` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1951-2041`): `conditional_collateral_t` debit is equal to `balances_t` credit. Both are in the 6-holding sum. Net change is zero.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:55:    *   `MarketSeedTx` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:2043-2090`): `balances_t` debit is equal to `conditional_collateral_t` credit. Both are in the 6-holding sum. Net change is zero.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:56:*   The integration test `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem` in `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:260` explicitly asserts this by calling `assert_total_ctf_conserved` pre- and post- a mint-then-redeem sequence.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:60:**5. Does `assert_complete_set_balanced` (MIN-semantics: `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`) hold after every transition?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:63:    *   `CompleteSetMintTx` / `MarketSeedTx`: Credit `collateral` by `N`, and credit `Σ_yes` and `Σ_no` by `N`. Post-state: `collateral = Σ_yes = Σ_no`, so `min(Σ_yes, Σ_no) == collateral`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:64:    *   `CompleteSetRedeemTx`: Assume `outcome=Yes`. Debit `collateral` by `M` and debit `Σ_yes` by `M`. `Σ_no` is unchanged. Post-state: `collateral' = collateral - M`, `Σ_yes' = Σ_yes - M`, `Σ_no' = Σ_no`. Since `collateral = Σ_yes` pre-redeem, `collateral' = Σ_yes'`. `Σ_no'` is now greater than `collateral'`. Thus, `min(Σ_yes', Σ_no') = Σ_yes' = collateral'`. The MIN-semantic correctly handles the asymmetry of the losing side's stranded shares.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:65:*   The recursive self-audit correctly identifies the necessity of the MIN-semantic, and integration tests (`halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`, `halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed`) confirm this invariant holds post-transition.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:66:*   **Finding**: The logic in all three dispatch arms correctly maintains the `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant under its MIN-semantic definition.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:69:**6. Can MarketSeedTx create liquidity without provider balance? (Must be rejected with `InsufficientBalanceForMint` or `InsufficientCollateral`.)**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:71:*   **Verification**: The `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:2051-2059` has two gates:
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:79:*   **Verification**: The `total_supply_micro` function in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:159-179` sums the values from `balances_t`, `escrows_t`, `stakes_t`, `challenge_cases_t`, and `conditional_collateral_t`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:80:*   The doc-comment at `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:173` explicitly states: `conditional_share_balances_t is INTENTIONALLY OMITTED per CR-13.3 + SG-13.2`. The code follows this comment.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:81:*   **Finding**: The implementation correctly treats conditional shares as claims, not Coin, by excluding `conditional_share_balances_t` from the total supply calculation.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:88:    2.  The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1990-1996` checks `if owned_units < redeem.share_amount.units { return Err(TransitionError::RedeemMoreThanOwned); }` *before* performing the subtraction at `src/state/sequencer.rs:2014`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:104:**10. Does CompleteSet schema extend cleanly to TB-14 PriceIndex (long/short interest derivable from `conditional_share_balances_t` aggregates)?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:116:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:122:**13. Is the MIN-semantics `assert_complete_set_balanced` invariant the right form (vs. strict equality), particularly for adversarial patterns?**

STEP_B ceremony argument** (Q-C from round-1, rebased): spec § 2.3 rebased the combined-ceremony argument from "Phase 0 minimum sufficient version is binding" (Codex r1 said advisory) to "functional coupling" (each half compile-error or no-op without other). handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:95:## Q7 STEP_B functional-coupling argument
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:122:**Single sentence**: close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform combined STEP_B ceremony adding a Sequencer entry-point on TuringBus + Kernel, and ship one substrate-independent CAS round-trip test — leaving transition function bodies + replay byte-identity to a future CO1.7.5 atom that depends on the Wave-2 substrate (CO P2.x family).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:152:2. **D3** — Combined STEP_B ceremony adds `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` field to `Kernel` + `submit_typed_tx` forwarder method on `TuringBus` (note: type is `TuringBus`, not `Bus`, per `src/bus.rs:53`). Sequencer instance lives in Kernel; TuringBus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9056:## Q7 STEP_B functional-coupling argument
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9058:Challenge. Functional coupling is stronger than the previous advisory wording, but STEP_B Phase 0 asks whether a less-invasive alternative exists (`handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:11-17`). One exists: put `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` on `TuringBus`, which already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. The spec must either move the field to TuringBus or explicitly defeat that alternative.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9125:## Q7 STEP_B functional-coupling argument
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9127:Challenge. Functional coupling is stronger than the previous advisory wording, but STEP_B Phase 0 asks whether a less-invasive alternative exists (`handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:11-17`). One exists: put `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` on `TuringBus`, which already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. The spec must either move the field to TuringBus or explicitly defeat that alternative.
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:40:## STEP_B_PROTOCOL_LEAKS
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:42:The plan's authoritative restricted-file list per D9 + § 5.4: `src/{bus,kernel,wal,ledger}.rs` plus `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` (per Codex audit § 5 step 4 inclusion).
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:45:- S1, S2, S3, S4: explicitly declare STEP_B per-atom for restricted files. ✓
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:46:- **S5: NO sprint-level STEP_B declaration**. Yet:
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:47:  - **S5.1 leaks**: touches `src/bus.rs` (restricted, per D9) — no STEP_B claim attached
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:48:  - **S5.6 leaks**: touches `src/wal.rs` (restricted, per D9) — no STEP_B claim attached
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:49:  - S5.4 touches `src/sdk/tools/search.rs` (NOT restricted per current CLAUDE.md, but borderline)
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:50:  - S5.5 touches `src/sdk/tools/librarian.rs` (NOT restricted per current CLAUDE.md)
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:51:- **S6.3 (TR migration)**: Trust Root commits per § 5.4 require external dual-audit always. The plan does declare PASS/PASS at S6.3. ✓ but it is not explicitly framed as STEP_B.
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:53:**STEP_B leakage list**: **S5.1, S5.6** — these atoms touch restricted files (`src/bus.rs`, `src/wal.rs`) without STEP_B claim. Plan should add an explicit "**STEP_B_PROTOCOL files touched in S5: `src/bus.rs` (S5.1), `src/wal.rs` (S5.6)**" footer matching S1/S2/S3/S4 sprint pattern.
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:72:2. **Add S5 sprint-level STEP_B declaration** matching S1-S4 pattern. S5.1 and S5.6 touch restricted files (`bus.rs`, `wal.rs`) and need explicit per-atom dual-audit framing.
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:97:The 5 residual V-violation gaps (V-06, V-09 explicit, V-17, V-20, V-23) are real but addressable with 5-7 additional atoms inserted into existing sprints. They do not require structural redesign. The S5 STEP_B leak is a documentation fix. None of the gaps invalidate Path B as the right architectural choice.
handover/audits/AUDITOR_TFR_PLAN_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:105:I am medium-high confidence on the V-coverage gap analysis (cross-verified against AUDITOR doc inventory table 24 rows). I am medium confidence on the heldout-leakage-via-genesis-commit risk (would need agent-tool access boundary analysis I did not perform). I am high confidence on the S5 STEP_B leak (sprint footers compared directly).

handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:62:Codex Q-7: STEP_B Phase 0 asks for less-invasive alternative; TuringBus already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. Codex Q-3 (M3c): saying Kernel "holds the driver, not the data" papers over Sequencer's heavy ownership of CAS + keypair + registries + writer + QState. Kernel `src/kernel.rs:5-6` explicit warning against domain-specific terms.
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:69:- STEP_B becomes single-file ceremony on `src/bus.rs` only
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:70:- Combined-ceremony justification (functional coupling) no longer needed; replaced with simpler "single restricted-file change" rationale
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:72:**Architectural side-benefit**: cleaner layering. TuringBus owns Kernel + Sequencer as peers. Kernel stays at pure-topology layer. Sequencer at runtime-orchestration layer. Single-file STEP_B ceremony is simpler than combined.
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:91:- ✅ STEP_B functional-coupling argument is stronger than minimum-sufficient-version invocation (Gemini Q4 PASS strong; Codex Q7 acknowledges-but-still-CHALLENGE because less-invasive alternative exists)

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:37:3.  **Strictly Better**: The v1.1 placement (`TuringBus.sequencer`) is strictly better than v1 (`Kernel.sequencer`). The v1 placement violated the Kernel's own documented "pure topology" doctrine and its explicit warning against domain-specific terms (`src/kernel.rs:5-6`). The v1.1 placement resolves this contradiction, leaves the Kernel UNTOUCHED, and simplifies the STEP_B ceremony to a single file (`src/bus.rs`), all of which are objective improvements.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:3:**CHALLENGE, conviction 5/5.** Repo HEAD verified as `c5059a5e3a63b8c5c1dbe079b748cf258792f0bd`. v2 resolves the r1 concerns at the architectural-intent level, but not at the substrate/compile-shape level. The two biggest blockers are concrete: `Sequencer.rejection_writer: Arc<RejectionEvidenceWriter>` cannot call `append_rejected` because the writer append API is `&mut self`, and the v2 escrow bridge uses `.contains_key()` directly on `EscrowsIndex` / `TaskMarketsIndex` newtypes that do not expose that method (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:226`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:106`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:133`). v2 should become a narrow v3 before STEP_B Phase-1 entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:62:**Revise preflight v3.** Do not abort the TB-2 charter: the target runtime gap remains real, `EscrowVault` non-use is enforceable, and the replay primitive is public. But v2 is not cleared for STEP_B Phase-1 because its P0-A/P0-B/P0-C/P0-D repairs still have compile-shape blockers at the source boundary. Phase-1 should start only after the v3 fixes above are incorporated.
handover/audits/CODEX_T_S_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:130:7. Only after steps 4-6 pass, start the bus/kernel split. Use STEP_B with Claude and Codex independent branches against the same spec. The losing branch or a fresh auditor reviews the winner per the post-fix protocol (`handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:168-195`).
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:56:The plan relies on the STEP_B protocol (two independent implementations by Claude and Codex) for `CO1.1.4` and `CO1.1.5`. This provides the opportunity for the strongest possible test: **differential fuzzing**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:58:The strongest test would be to build a harness that generates a high volume of valid, randomized `(Q_t, tx_i)` inputs and feeds them to both the Claude and Codex implementations simultaneously. Any divergence in the byte-level output of `(Q_{t+1}, signals_t)` between the two branches indicates either a bug in one or both implementations, or an ambiguity in the spec itself. This is far stronger than a simple unit test suite. The plan should be amended to make differential fuzzing the primary acceptance criterion for the STEP_B atoms.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:78:The 4th overshoot is most likely to land in **CO1.1.4 / CO1.1.5 (the bus/kernel split)**. This was already the highest-risk part of the plan. Even with a formal spec, the practical difficulty of refactoring a complex, stateful monolith is immense. The process of integrating and debugging two independent implementations (STEP_B) and resolving the inevitable subtle discrepancies discovered by differential fuzzing will almost certainly take longer than the 1.5 weeks allocated to each. The spec reduces ambiguity, but it does not eliminate implementation complexity.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:108:2.  **Testing Strength**: The plan now hinges on the quality of conformance tests derived from the spec. As noted in Q6, a weak test suite renders the spec useless as a gate. The project needs to adopt a stronger testing philosophy, with differential fuzzing for STEP_B atoms as a non-negotiable requirement.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:4. **STEP_B-restricted touch surface**: src/bus.rs + src/kernel.rs (currently use legacy crate::ledger; CO1.7.5 D3 adds Sequencer wiring). Hygiene OBS sedimented at `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:55:**Q-C. D3 combined STEP_B ceremony (§ 1 D3)**: spec rejects per-file STEP_B (Q3 closed) in favor of one A/B unit covering kernel.rs + bus.rs together. Justification: "Bus forwarder is meaningless without Kernel field; STEP_B Phase 0 minimum-sufficient version".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:56:- Sound? Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, is "minimum sufficient" a binding criterion for ceremony scoping, or is it advisory?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:90:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:121:**Single sentence**: implement the 7 per-kind transition function bodies (currently `Err(NotYetImplemented)` stubs), close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` wiring after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for the bus.rs / kernel.rs Sequencer entry-point, and un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` so the byte-identity I-DETHASH witness fires end-to-end.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:139:3. **D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony** for `src/kernel.rs` (Sequencer field) + `src/bus.rs` (forwarder method) as one A/B unit. Sequencer instance lives in Kernel only; Bus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`. Coexists with legacy `crate::ledger`; full retirement is CO1.1.5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:253:### D3 — STEP_B ceremony for runtime Sequencer entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:291:**STEP_B ceremony — combined (bus.rs + kernel.rs as one unit)**: per `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version", the bus.rs forwarder cannot be reasoned about without the kernel.rs field it forwards through. Splitting into two independent ceremonies would break the necessity-audit's "what observable behavior is broken now" question (each half is meaningless in isolation). Therefore:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:298:- `src/kernel.rs` (additive: 1 field + 1 constructor variant) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:299:- `src/bus.rs` (additive: 1 forwarder method, NO struct field) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:300:- `src/state/sequencer.rs` (D2 head_t close, D1 dispatch_transition forwarders) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:301:- `src/state/transitions/{step,verify,challenge,reuse,finalize_reward,task_expire,terminal_summary}.rs` (NEW; 7 files) + `mod.rs` — NOT STEP_B-restricted

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1038:## § 9 STEP_B disposition (K4 corrected typo)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1047:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1049:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1825:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1854:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2246:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2427:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2480:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2529:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2540:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2591:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2599:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7855:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7861:- Memory updates. `feedback_step_b_protocol` memory is path-agnostic; references "restricted files" semantically, no edit needed.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8076:    32	3. **D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony** for `src/kernel.rs` (Sequencer field) + `src/bus.rs` (forwarder method) as one A/B unit. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8190:   146	### D3 — STEP_B ceremony for runtime Sequencer entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8228:   184	**STEP_B ceremony — combined (bus.rs + kernel.rs as one unit)**: per `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version", the bus.rs forwarder cannot be reasoned about without the kernel.rs field it forwards through. Splitting into two independent ceremonies would break the necessity-audit's "what observable behavior is broken now" question (each half is meaningless in isolation). Therefore:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8235:   191	- `src/kernel.rs` (additive: 1 field + 1 constructor variant) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8236:   192	- `src/bus.rs` (additive: 1 forwarder method, NO struct field) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8237:   193	- `src/state/sequencer.rs` (D2 head_t close, D1 dispatch_transition forwarders) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8242:   198	**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10076:   322	**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10078:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10494:   714	// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10740:   293	> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10806:   948	**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11501:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6670:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11629:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:25:**Mandate**: round-1 dual external audit on the **CO1.7-impl bundle** (A1 + A2 + A3 + A4) + **CO1.4-extra** atom, all mid-implementation commits. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + spec § 12: this PASS/PASS gates entry to CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11636:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:49:- **PASS/PASS** unblocks **CO1.7.5** (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs); CO1.7.5 is the final L4 atom

Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15238:handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md:28:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17468:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4809:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17788:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:198:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:18062:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7855:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:18380:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11685:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:671:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:18498:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11856:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11749:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19078:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13732:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20560:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20561:Combined ceremony is permissible, but the spec overstates the protocol. STEP_B Phase 0 asks auditors for the “minimum sufficient version” (`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:9-17`); it does not bind ceremony scoping by itself. A combined A/B unit is still reasonable because `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` are coupled.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20606:- P5-P12 are materially present (`spec:359-366`, `CLAUDE.md:14`, `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`). P3 is stale wording: it references “§ 6 ack #8” but §6 now has 6 items.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20631:3. Fix D3 wiring spec to compile against actual `TuringBus`/`Kernel` derives, or move Sequencer ownership to a runtime layer with a precise STEP_B target.

STEP_B Phase 0 asks auditors for the “minimum sufficient version” (`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:9-17`); it does not bind ceremony scoping by itself. A combined A/B unit is still reasonable because `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` are coupled.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5194:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3822:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6239:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:66:| **TFR — TuringOS Foundation Refactor** | Path B real git substrate per Art. 0.4 user decision | 🚀 **LAUNCHED 2026-04-26** | 7-sprint plan (S0–S6) ~7-10 wk; ~50 atoms; STEP_B per atom on bus/kernel/wal/ledger; dual external audit per sprint exit + per STEP_B atom; gix (pure Rust) substrate; per-cell runtime git repo; Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩ literally. Plan: `handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md`. PREREG arc extension proposed (+70d cap; § 9 Proposal A). Pending: user Q1-Q10 decisions + S0 exit dual audit PASS/PASS. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6657:./handover/ai-direct/PAPER_1_v2_DRAFT_SKELETON_2026-04-24.md:197:Node-count distribution per solve (addressing P2-12): to be extracted from gp_payload when Appendix C is finalized; preliminary check shows some B solves terminate in 1 `step` node (multi-line inline proof), others in multi-node chains. The solve-count claim in § 4.1 is robust to this distinction; the "multi-agent collaboration" interpretation is restricted to multi-node chains and is explicitly scoped in § 7 Limitation 5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6949:./handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:460:**STEP_B_PROTOCOL files touched in S2**: `src/wal.rs` (S2.3), `src/ledger.rs` (S2.1, S2.2). Per-atom dual-audit on these two atoms specifically.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6968:./handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:566:Math: ~50 atoms across the arc. STEP_B atoms = ~25. Per-atom external audits = ~25; per-sprint exit audits = 7. Total external audit invocations ≈ 32, at ~30min each = 16 wall-hours of audit-runtime, plus fix-cycle latency. Budget envelope per audit-cycle is ~$8–15 USD at our backbone pricing → ~$300–500 total external audit spend across TFR. Within PREREG § 11 USD-500 cap, but tight — this is one of the open questions in § 10.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7023:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:5:**Principle**: real data decides, not opinion. Parallel branch lets us test change empirically before merging.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7024:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:14:- What's the *minimum sufficient* version? (avoid scope creep)

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7027:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:59:- **Sample size justification**: binomial approximation — a 3-solve difference (6 pp on N=50) is ~2σ above null; 6-solve difference (12 pp) is ~4σ. Pre-register **strict-win threshold = ΔSolveRate ≥ 3** (consistent with v3.1 decision rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7028:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:61:- **Decision rule**:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7029:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:87:External auditors see **data only** (no researcher interpretation). They apply the pre-registered decision rule and return PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7791:./handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:225:CO1.7-extra now touches a single STEP_B-restricted file: `src/bus.rs`. No combined-ceremony justification needed. Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, the change is "minimum sufficient version" and has no less-invasive alternative (the typed-tx submission path needs SOME entry-point in the runtime layer; TuringBus is the canonical orchestrator).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7808:./handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:505:STEP_B Branch B re-derivation performed in a separate session against `5ce01b1` (Branch A). Empirical finding: spec § 2.1 was precise enough to converge two independent derivers on **byte-identical executable Rust** (imports, struct field, constructor delegate, forwarder match logic) but **NOT** on literal full-file byte-identity — divergences appeared in (a) doc-comment prose paraphrase, (b) `with_sequencer`/`submit_typed_tx` parameter line-wrapping, and (c) `submit_typed_tx` method placement order within `impl TuringBus`. Pure `cargo fmt` normalization on both branches did NOT reduce divergence to zero (it preserves valid pre-existing wrapping + comment text + method ordering).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7811:./handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:533:- **B3** (Codex Q4): removed stale Kernel references at preface line 14 (single-sentence summary now reads "single-file STEP_B ceremony adding a Sequencer entry-point on TuringBus (Kernel UNTOUCHED)") + § 6 pre-implementation gate (removed `src/kernel.rs` from gate file list; explicitly noted "Kernel UNTOUCHED").

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7998:./handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md:28:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:651:## § 9 STEP_B disposition (K4 corrected typo)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:653:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:660:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:662:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2432:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3624:## Q-F STEP_B disposition
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3674:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3995:## § 9 STEP_B disposition
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3997:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4778:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4807:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4809:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5199:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5380:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5433:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5482:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5493:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5544:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5552:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5664:// Only callable from these two paths, enforced by `pub(restricted)` + cargo-deny
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5665:pub(restricted = predicate_runner, terminal_summary_emitter)
