handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:325:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1428:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:326:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1749:## § 9 STEP_B disposition
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:327:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1751:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:328:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2532:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:329:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2561:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:330:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2563:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:331:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2953:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:332:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3134:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:333:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3187:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:334:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3236:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:335:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3247:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:348:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6787:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3:**Scope**: any change to files in CLAUDE.md's restricted list (currently `kernel.rs`, `bus.rs`, `wallet.rs`). Also applicable to any proposal that touches "institution" per C-031.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:349:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6788:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:13:- Is a less-invasive alternative available? (same effect, no restricted-file touch)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:350:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6789:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:17:**Gate**: if both auditors say "less-invasive alternative exists", take that path instead. If both say "change is necessary", proceed to Phase 1. If they disagree, take the conservative verdict per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:351:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6790:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:107:1. **C-010**: external audit at 3 junctures (necessity, diff, verdict). Researcher cannot self-approve a restricted change.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:354:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6911:    14	- `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` 改动走 STEP_B_PROTOCOL（不直接编辑 main）
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:357:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7361:293:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:358:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7368:322:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:359:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7369:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:360:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7391:948:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:392:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:9640:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:473:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:45:## Q4 Combined STEP_B argument rebase
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:474:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:48:-   **Functional-coupling criterion**: This is a much stronger and more objective criterion than the original "minimum sufficient version" argument, which `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` correctly frames as advisory. Functional coupling—where each half of a change is a compile-error or a no-op without the other—provides a rigorous, machine-checkable justification for treating the changes as a single atomic unit. This strengthens the integrity of the STEP_B process.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:475:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:50:-   **Generalizability**: This justification is not a one-off. The principle of functional coupling can and should be used as the standard for justifying future combined STEP_B ceremonies. It provides a clear test: if the changes can be landed and tested independently without being dead code, they should be separate ceremonies. If not, they can be combined.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:476:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:72:-   **D3 compile-coherence**: The STEP_B ceremony itself serves as the test for the compile-time coherence of the D3 wiring graph. No additional test is required for this part.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:483:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:21:**Q5. STEP_B atom risk:** The designation of `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` splits as parallel branches under the STEP_B protocol is a sophisticated and appropriate response to a high-risk refactoring task. While a sequential refactor might seem "safer," it would be significantly slower and forfeit the immense value of a second, independent implementation from Codex. The parallel approach, governed by the Tri-Model Protocol's cross-comparison and tie-breaker mechanism, is a superior strategy for mitigating risk in these architecturally critical atoms. It trades simple safety for robust, verified correctness. **[PASS]**

STEP_B ceremony argument** (Q-C from round-1, rebased): spec § 2.3 rebased the combined-ceremony argument from "Phase 0 minimum sufficient version is binding" (Codex r1 said advisory) to "functional coupling" (each half compile-error or no-op without other). Is functional-coupling a stronger criterion that justifies combined ceremony, or is it an alternative way to phrase the same advisory request?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:611:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:95:## Q7 STEP_B functional-coupling argument
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:612:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:122:**Single sentence**: close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform combined STEP_B ceremony adding a Sequencer entry-point on TuringBus + Kernel, and ship one substrate-independent CAS round-trip test — leaving transition function bodies + replay byte-identity to a future CO1.7.5 atom that depends on the Wave-2 substrate (CO P2.x family).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:613:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:152:2. **D3** — Combined STEP_B ceremony adds `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` field to `Kernel` + `submit_typed_tx` forwarder method on `TuringBus` (note: type is `TuringBus`, not `Bus`, per `src/bus.rs:53`). Sequencer instance lives in Kernel; TuringBus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:614:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:237:## § 2 D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony for runtime entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:615:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:304:Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, "minimum sufficient version" is technically **advisory** language asking auditors to favor the smallest change that works. CO1.7-extra rests the combined-ceremony argument on **functional coupling** (a stronger criterion):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:616:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:309:Each half is a no-op without the other. A/B byte-identity testing each half independently would test two non-functional changes; combining them into one A/B unit tests the actual minimum-functional change. This is a **stronger** application of STEP_B's spirit than the per-file alternative.

Also adds the `#[derive(Debug)]` on Sequencer in `src/state/sequencer.rs` (NOT STEP_B-restricted; landed alongside the ceremony for compile coherence).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:661:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9058:Challenge. Functional coupling is stronger than the previous advisory wording, but STEP_B Phase 0 asks whether a less-invasive alternative exists (`handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:11-17`). One exists: put `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` on `TuringBus`, which already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. The spec must either move the field to TuringBus or explicitly defeat that alternative.

But B7 should Trust-Root `cost_aggregator.rs`, `wall_clock.rs`, `post_hoc_verifier.rs`, and likely `evaluator.rs`, `jsonl_schema.rs`, and `llm_http.rs`; current PREREG §1.8 list omits them.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:854:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:62:Codex Q-7: STEP_B Phase 0 asks for less-invasive alternative; TuringBus already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. Codex Q-3 (M3c): saying Kernel "holds the driver, not the data" papers over Sequencer's heavy ownership of CAS + keypair + registries + writer + QState. Kernel `src/kernel.rs:5-6` explicit warning against domain-specific terms.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:855:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:69:- STEP_B becomes single-file ceremony on `src/bus.rs` only
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:857:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:72:**Architectural side-benefit**: cleaner layering. TuringBus owns Kernel + Sequencer as peers. Kernel stays at pure-topology layer. Sequencer at runtime-orchestration layer. Single-file STEP_B ceremony is simpler than combined.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:858:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R2_2026-04-29.md:91:- ✅ STEP_B functional-coupling argument is stronger than minimum-sufficient-version invocation (Gemini Q4 PASS strong; Codex Q7 acknowledges-but-still-CHALLENGE because less-invasive alternative exists)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:862:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:3936:**Protocol for restricted-file changes**: `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` (necessity audit → parallel branch → A/B statistical test → merge on empirical win only). Do NOT directly edit restricted files even with authorization; always A/B test.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:865:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:3942:| WAL emission | `src/kernel.rs` or bus.rs | F-2026-04-15-03 | **Human confirm + Step-B** | STEP_B_PROTOCOL |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:866:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:37:3.  **Strictly Better**: The v1.1 placement (`TuringBus.sequencer`) is strictly better than v1 (`Kernel.sequencer`). The v1 placement violated the Kernel's own documented "pure topology" doctrine and its explicit warning against domain-specific terms (`src/kernel.rs:5-6`). The v1.1 placement resolves this contradiction, leaves the Kernel UNTOUCHED, and simplifies the STEP_B ceremony to a single file (`src/bus.rs`), all of which are objective improvements.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:867:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:3:**CHALLENGE, conviction 5/5.** Repo HEAD verified as `c5059a5e3a63b8c5c1dbe079b748cf258792f0bd`. v2 resolves the r1 concerns at the architectural-intent level, but not at the substrate/compile-shape level. The two biggest blockers are concrete: `Sequencer.rejection_writer: Arc<RejectionEvidenceWriter>` cannot call `append_rejected` because the writer append API is `&mut self`, and the v2 escrow bridge uses `.contains_key()` directly on `EscrowsIndex` / `TaskMarketsIndex` newtypes that do not expose that method (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:226`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:106`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:133`). v2 should become a narrow v3 before STEP_B Phase-1 entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:868:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:62:**Revise preflight v3.** Do not abort the TB-2 charter: the target runtime gap remains real, `EscrowVault` non-use is enforceable, and the replay primitive is public. But v2 is not cleared for STEP_B Phase-1 because its P0-A/P0-B/P0-C/P0-D repairs still have compile-shape blockers at the source boundary. Phase-1 should start only after the v3 fixes above are incorporated.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:869:handover/audits/CODEX_T_S_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:130:7. Only after steps 4-6 pass, start the bus/kernel split. Use STEP_B with Claude and Codex independent branches against the same spec. The losing branch or a fresh auditor reviews the winner per the post-fix protocol (`handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:168-195`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:870:handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:56:The plan relies on the STEP_B protocol (two independent implementations by Claude and Codex) for `CO1.1.4` and `CO1.1.5`. This provides the opportunity for the strongest possible test: **differential fuzzing**.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:871:handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:58:The strongest test would be to build a harness that generates a high volume of valid, randomized `(Q_t, tx_i)` inputs and feeds them to both the Claude and Codex implementations simultaneously. Any divergence in the byte-level output of `(Q_{t+1}, signals_t)` between the two branches indicates either a bug in one or both implementations, or an ambiguity in the spec itself. This is far stronger than a simple unit test suite. The plan should be amended to make differential fuzzing the primary acceptance criterion for the STEP_B atoms.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:872:handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:78:The 4th overshoot is most likely to land in **CO1.1.4 / CO1.1.5 (the bus/kernel split)**. This was already the highest-risk part of the plan. Even with a formal spec, the practical difficulty of refactoring a complex, stateful monolith is immense. The process of integrating and debugging two independent implementations (STEP_B) and resolving the inevitable subtle discrepancies discovered by differential fuzzing will almost certainly take longer than the 1.5 weeks allocated to each. The spec reduces ambiguity, but it does not eliminate implementation complexity.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:873:handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:108:2.  **Testing Strength**: The plan now hinges on the quality of conformance tests derived from the spec. As noted in Q6, a weak test suite renders the spec useless as a gate. The project needs to adopt a stronger testing philosophy, with differential fuzzing for STEP_B atoms as a non-negotiable requirement.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:874:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:4. **STEP_B-restricted touch surface**: src/bus.rs + src/kernel.rs (currently use legacy crate::ledger; CO1.7.5 D3 adds Sequencer wiring). Hygiene OBS sedimented at `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:875:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:55:**Q-C. D3 combined STEP_B ceremony (§ 1 D3)**: spec rejects per-file STEP_B (Q3 closed) in favor of one A/B unit covering kernel.rs + bus.rs together. Justification: "Bus forwarder is meaningless without Kernel field; STEP_B Phase 0 minimum-sufficient version".
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:876:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:56:- Sound? Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, is "minimum sufficient" a binding criterion for ceremony scoping, or is it advisory?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:877:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:90:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:878:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:121:**Single sentence**: implement the 7 per-kind transition function bodies (currently `Err(NotYetImplemented)` stubs), close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` wiring after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for the bus.rs / kernel.rs Sequencer entry-point, and un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` so the byte-identity I-DETHASH witness fires end-to-end.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:879:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:139:3. **D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony** for `src/kernel.rs` (Sequencer field) + `src/bus.rs` (forwarder method) as one A/B unit. Sequencer instance lives in Kernel only; Bus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`. Coexists with legacy `crate::ledger`; full retirement is CO1.1.5.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:880:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:253:### D3 — STEP_B ceremony for runtime Sequencer entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:881:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:291:**STEP_B ceremony — combined (bus.rs + kernel.rs as one unit)**: per `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version", the bus.rs forwarder cannot be reasoned about without the kernel.rs field it forwards through. Splitting into two independent ceremonies would break the necessity-audit's "what observable behavior is broken now" question (each half is meaningless in isolation). Therefore:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:882:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:298:- `src/kernel.rs` (additive: 1 field + 1 constructor variant) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:883:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:299:- `src/bus.rs` (additive: 1 forwarder method, NO struct field) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:884:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:300:- `src/state/sequencer.rs` (D2 head_t close, D1 dispatch_transition forwarders) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:885:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:301:- `src/state/transitions/{step,verify,challenge,reuse,finalize_reward,task_expire,terminal_summary}.rs` (NEW; 7 files) + `mod.rs` — NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:886:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:302:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (D2 trait extension: 1 default method) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:887:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:305:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:888:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:342:| **Q3 STEP_B per file vs combined** | **CLOSED**: combined ceremony covering bus.rs + kernel.rs as one A/B unit (§ 1 D3). | Per-file ceremony fails STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version" — the bus.rs forwarder is meaningless without the kernel.rs field it forwards through; A/B byte-identity check is only meaningful on the coupled change. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:889:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:399:  - D3 STEP_B combined ceremony (kernel.rs + bus.rs): ~30-60 LoC per branch × 2 A/B branches = ~60-120 LoC of total restricted-file delta
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:890:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:411:4. **Combined STEP_B ceremony** (per Q3 closure): one A/B unit covers `src/bus.rs` + `src/kernel.rs` together because the bus.rs forwarder is meaningless without kernel.rs's Sequencer field. Per-file ceremony was rejected for failing STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version".
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:891:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:412:5. **`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` and `src/wal.rs` untouched**: smoke S5 verifies 0 hits in wallet; spec § 9 of CO1_7 v1.2 separately confirms wal.rs is not STEP_B-restricted. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented + fixed in `OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke; the restricted set now reads `src/{kernel,bus}.rs + src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:892:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:427:| S5 | "wallet (at `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) is untouched" (§ 1 D3) | `grep -c 'transition_ledger\|state::sequencer\|TypedTx' src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` | 0 hits. Note: `src/wallet.rs` does NOT exist at HEAD `2f5093a` (CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path is stale; logged as OBS post-PASS/PASS — see § 1 D3 closing paragraph). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:893:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:450:| S5 | wallet untouched | `grep -c 'transition_ledger\|state::sequencer\|TypedTx' src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` (NOT `src/wallet.rs` — that path does not exist) | `0` | ✅ PASS (with path correction; CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift sedimented as separate OBS post-PASS/PASS) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:894:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:468:- P7: § 1 D3 architecture simplification — Sequencer lives in Kernel only (not Bus); Bus exposes thin forwarder `submit_typed_tx` via `self.kernel.sequencer`; **combined STEP_B ceremony** (one A/B unit covers both files) replaces per-file ceremony — justified by STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version" since the Bus forwarder is meaningless without the Kernel field
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:895:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:473:- P12: hygiene fix landed `CLAUDE.md:14` + `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3` to correct path drift; `OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` records rationale
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:896:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:479:3. then CO1.7.5-impl (D1 + D2 + D3 STEP_B + D4)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:897:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:563:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:898:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1038:## § 9 STEP_B disposition (K4 corrected typo)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:899:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:900:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1047:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:901:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1049:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:902:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1825:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:903:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1854:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:904:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:905:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2246:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:906:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2427:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:907:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2480:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:908:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2529:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:909:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2540:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:910:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2591:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:911:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2599:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:930:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8058:    14	**Single sentence**: implement the 7 per-kind transition function bodies (currently `Err(NotYetImplemented)` stubs), close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` wiring after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for the bus.rs / kernel.rs Sequencer entry-point, and un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` so the byte-identity I-DETHASH witness fires end-to-end.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:931:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8076:    32	3. **D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony** for `src/kernel.rs` (Sequencer field) + `src/bus.rs` (forwarder method) as one A/B unit. Sequencer instance lives in Kernel only; Bus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`. Coexists with legacy `crate::ledger`; full retirement is CO1.1.5.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:932:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8190:   146	### D3 — STEP_B ceremony for runtime Sequencer entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:933:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8228:   184	**STEP_B ceremony — combined (bus.rs + kernel.rs as one unit)**: per `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version", the bus.rs forwarder cannot be reasoned about without the kernel.rs field it forwards through. Splitting into two independent ceremonies would break the necessity-audit's "what observable behavior is broken now" question (each half is meaningless in isolation). Therefore:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:934:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8235:   191	- `src/kernel.rs` (additive: 1 field + 1 constructor variant) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:936:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8237:   193	- `src/state/sequencer.rs` (D2 head_t close, D1 dispatch_transition forwarders) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:937:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8238:   194	- `src/state/transitions/{step,verify,challenge,reuse,finalize_reward,task_expire,terminal_summary}.rs` (NEW; 7 files) + `mod.rs` — NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:938:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8239:   195	- `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (D2 trait extension: 1 default method) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:939:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8242:   198	**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:951:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10076:   322	**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:952:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10078:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:953:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10494:   714	// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:954:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10740:   293	> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:955:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10806:   948	**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:956:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10891:    78	- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:977:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11501:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6670:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:978:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11629:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:25:**Mandate**: round-1 dual external audit on the **CO1.7-impl bundle** (A1 + A2 + A3 + A4) + **CO1.4-extra** atom, all mid-implementation commits. handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:1015:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:12575:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7955:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:1022:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13585:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R3_2026-04-28.md:64:3. **CO1.7.5+ wiring** (separate atom): `head_t` mutation when `Git2LedgerWriter` returns commit_sha; integration into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` (THIS will need STEP_B per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:1023:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13620:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:4. **STEP_B-restricted touch surface**: src/bus.rs + src/kernel.rs (currently use legacy crate::ledger; CO1.7.5 D3 adds Sequencer wiring). Estimated effort: 15 minutes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:62:**Revise preflight v3.** Do not abort the TB-2 charter: the target runtime gap remains real, `EscrowVault` non-use is enforceable, and the replay primitive is public. But v2 is not cleared for STEP_B Phase-1 because its P0-A/P0-B/P0-C/P0-D repairs still have compile-shape blockers at the source boundary. Phase-1 should start only after the v3 fixes above are incorporated.
handover/audits/CODEX_T_S_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:130:7. Only after steps 4-6 pass, start the bus/kernel split. Use STEP_B with Claude and Codex independent branches against the same spec. The losing branch or a fresh auditor reviews the winner per the post-fix protocol (`handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:168-195`).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:36:**Finding**: PASS. The operation is a balance↔collateral migration. The `CompleteSetMintTx` dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1808` correctly debits `balances_t` and credits `conditional_collateral_t` by the same `amount`. The extended `assert_total_ctf_conserved` invariant in `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:205` correctly includes `conditional_collateral_t` in the 6-holding sum, ensuring the total supply is bit-for-bit conserved.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:37:**Adversarial Note**: An initial vulnerability existed where a negative `amount` (`MicroCoin` is `i64`) would credit the user's balance while creating negative collateral and a huge `u128` share balance. The recursive self-audit's remediation log (`RECURSIVE_AUDIT...md` §12.3) confirms this was fixed by changing the check from `== 0` to `<= 0` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1816`. The remediated implementation is correct.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:40:**Finding**: PASS. No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` performs a state lookup on `task_markets_t`. If the state is `Open` or `Expired`, it is correctly rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` in `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:49:**Finding**: PASS. Yes. The MIN-semantics implementation at `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:289` is the correct and robust form of the invariant, correctly handling the post-resolution state where losing-side shares are stranded but the winning side remains fully collateralized. The recursive self-audit (`RECURSIVE_AUDIT...md` §12.3) notes that this assertion is now called from every relevant dispatch arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs` post-remediation, ensuring live enforcement.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:52:**Finding**: PASS. No. The `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1959` correctly gates on `balances_t[provider] >= collateral_amount`, rejecting with `InsufficientBalanceForMint`. This is confirmed by test `sg_13_3_market_seed_fails_if_provider_lacks_balance`. The negative-amount attack vector is also closed by the `<= 0` check at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1953` per the VETO remediation.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:58:**Finding**: PASS. No. Per `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1105`, `ShareAmount` is a `u128` newtype, preventing negative values at the type level. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1907` performs a `owned_units < redeem.share_amount.units` check before debiting, rejecting with `RedeemMoreThanOwned` and preventing underflow.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:77:**Finding**: PASS. Yes. The MIN-semantics invariant is correct and more robust than a strict equality check on both sides. It correctly models the post-resolution state where losing-side shares become unbacked claims. My primary adversarial concern was minting/seeding into an already-resolved event to grief the chain state. The recursive self-audit's remediation log (`RECURSIVE_AUDIT...md` §12.3, Gemini Q13 fix) confirms that an `EventNotOpen` gate was added to the `CompleteSetMintTx` and `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arms at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1823` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:1959` respectively. This closes the attack vector and makes the overall design robust.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:56:The plan relies on the STEP_B protocol (two independent implementations by Claude and Codex) for `CO1.1.4` and `CO1.1.5`. This provides the opportunity for the strongest possible test: **differential fuzzing**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:58:The strongest test would be to build a harness that generates a high volume of valid, randomized `(Q_t, tx_i)` inputs and feeds them to both the Claude and Codex implementations simultaneously. Any divergence in the byte-level output of `(Q_{t+1}, signals_t)` between the two branches indicates either a bug in one or both implementations, or an ambiguity in the spec itself. This is far stronger than a simple unit test suite. The plan should be amended to make differential fuzzing the primary acceptance criterion for the STEP_B atoms.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:78:The 4th overshoot is most likely to land in **CO1.1.4 / CO1.1.5 (the bus/kernel split)**. This was already the highest-risk part of the plan. Even with a formal spec, the practical difficulty of refactoring a complex, stateful monolith is immense. The process of integrating and debugging two independent implementations (STEP_B) and resolving the inevitable subtle discrepancies discovered by differential fuzzing will almost certainly take longer than the 1.5 weeks allocated to each. The spec reduces ambiguity, but it does not eliminate implementation complexity.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:108:2.  **Testing Strength**: The plan now hinges on the quality of conformance tests derived from the spec. As noted in Q6, a weak test suite renders the spec useless as a gate. The project needs to adopt a stronger testing philosophy, with differential fuzzing for STEP_B atoms as a non-negotiable requirement.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8946:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:8332:handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:17:**Gate**: if both auditors say "less-invasive alternative exists", take that path instead. If both say "change is necessary", proceed to Phase 1. If they disagree, take the conservative verdict per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9867:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:17:**Gate**: if both auditors say "less-invasive alternative exists", take that path instead. If both say "change is necessary", proceed to Phase 1. If they disagree, take the conservative verdict per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:34:3. **Shipped code (target of D1-D4)**: src/state/sequencer.rs (apply_one + dispatch_transition stubs), src/state/typed_tx.rs (frozen TypedTx + 22 TransitionError + 4 SignalKind), src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs (Git2LedgerWriter + InMemoryLedgerWriter), src/state/q_state.rs (Q_t with NodeId tuple struct)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:4. **STEP_B-restricted touch surface**: src/bus.rs + src/kernel.rs (currently use legacy crate::ledger; CO1.7.5 D3 adds Sequencer wiring). Hygiene OBS sedimented at `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:55:**Q-C. D3 combined STEP_B ceremony (§ 1 D3)**: spec rejects per-file STEP_B (Q3 closed) in favor of one A/B unit covering kernel.rs + bus.rs together. Justification: "Bus forwarder is meaningless without Kernel field; STEP_B Phase 0 minimum-sufficient version".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:56:- Sound? Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, is "minimum sufficient" a binding criterion for ceremony scoping, or is it advisory?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:90:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:121:**Single sentence**: implement the 7 per-kind transition function bodies (currently `Err(NotYetImplemented)` stubs), close the G-1 carry-forward `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` wiring after `Git2LedgerWriter.commit`, perform STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for the bus.rs / kernel.rs Sequencer entry-point, and un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` so the byte-identity I-DETHASH witness fires end-to-end.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:139:3. **D3 — Combined STEP_B ceremony** for `src/kernel.rs` (Sequencer field) + `src/bus.rs` (forwarder method) as one A/B unit. Sequencer instance lives in Kernel only; Bus forwards via `self.kernel.sequencer`. Coexists with legacy `crate::ledger`; full retirement is CO1.1.5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:185:Replace each `Err(TransitionError::NotYetImplemented)` arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs::dispatch_transition` (lines 53-61) with a real pure transition function. Each body lives in its OWN file under `src/state/transitions/` (NEW directory; one file per variant) and is called by `dispatch_transition` via a single-line forwarder. Authoritative pseudocode source: `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC v1.4`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:253:### D3 — STEP_B ceremony for runtime Sequencer entry-point
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:291:**STEP_B ceremony — combined (bus.rs + kernel.rs as one unit)**: per `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version", the bus.rs forwarder cannot be reasoned about without the kernel.rs field it forwards through. Splitting into two independent ceremonies would break the necessity-audit's "what observable behavior is broken now" question (each half is meaningless in isolation). Therefore:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:298:- `src/kernel.rs` (additive: 1 field + 1 constructor variant) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:299:- `src/bus.rs` (additive: 1 forwarder method, NO struct field) — STEP_B-restricted; covered by combined ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:300:- `src/state/sequencer.rs` (D2 head_t close, D1 dispatch_transition forwarders) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:301:- `src/state/transitions/{step,verify,challenge,reuse,finalize_reward,task_expire,terminal_summary}.rs` (NEW; 7 files) + `mod.rs` — NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:302:- `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (D2 trait extension: 1 default method) — additive; NOT STEP_B-restricted
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:305:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:342:| **Q3 STEP_B per file vs combined** | **CLOSED**: combined ceremony covering bus.rs + kernel.rs as one A/B unit (§ 1 D3). | Per-file ceremony fails STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version" — the bus.rs forwarder is meaningless without the kernel.rs field it forwards through; A/B byte-identity check is only meaningful on the coupled change. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:389:**Pre-implementation gate**: spec must reach PASS/PASS before any code in `src/state/transitions/`, `src/bus.rs`, `src/kernel.rs`, or `src/state/sequencer.rs` D2 lines is written. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:399:  - D3 STEP_B combined ceremony (kernel.rs + bus.rs): ~30-60 LoC per branch × 2 A/B branches = ~60-120 LoC of total restricted-file delta
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:411:4. **Combined STEP_B ceremony** (per Q3 closure): one A/B unit covers `src/bus.rs` + `src/kernel.rs` together because the bus.rs forwarder is meaningless without kernel.rs's Sequencer field. Per-file ceremony was rejected for failing STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:412:5. **`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` and `src/wal.rs` untouched**: smoke S5 verifies 0 hits in wallet; spec § 9 of CO1_7 v1.2 separately confirms wal.rs is not STEP_B-restricted. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented + fixed in `OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke; the restricted set now reads `src/{kernel,bus}.rs + src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:423:| S1 | "every variant returns `Err(TransitionError::NotYetImplemented)`" (§ 0.1) | `grep -c 'NotYetImplemented' src/state/sequencer.rs` lines 53-61 | exactly 7 hits |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:427:| S5 | "wallet (at `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) is untouched" (§ 1 D3) | `grep -c 'transition_ledger\|state::sequencer\|TypedTx' src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` | 0 hits. Note: `src/wallet.rs` does NOT exist at HEAD `2f5093a` (CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path is stale; logged as OBS post-PASS/PASS — see § 1 D3 closing paragraph). |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:446:| S1 | 7× NotYetImplemented in dispatch_transition lines 53-61 | `sed -n '53,61p' src/state/sequencer.rs \| grep -c NotYetImplemented` | `7` | ✅ PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:450:| S5 | wallet untouched | `grep -c 'transition_ledger\|state::sequencer\|TypedTx' src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` (NOT `src/wallet.rs` — that path does not exist) | `0` | ✅ PASS (with path correction; CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift sedimented as separate OBS post-PASS/PASS) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:468:- P7: § 1 D3 architecture simplification — Sequencer lives in Kernel only (not Bus); Bus exposes thin forwarder `submit_typed_tx` via `self.kernel.sequencer`; **combined STEP_B ceremony** (one A/B unit covers both files) replaces per-file ceremony — justified by STEP_B Phase 0 "minimum sufficient version" since the Bus forwarder is meaningless without the Kernel field
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:473:- P12: hygiene fix landed `CLAUDE.md:14` + `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3` to correct path drift; `OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` records rationale
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:479:3. then CO1.7.5-impl (D1 + D2 + D3 STEP_B + D4)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:563:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:740:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1038:## § 9 STEP_B disposition (K4 corrected typo)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1047:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1049:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1825:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1854:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2246:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2427:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2480:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2529:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2540:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2591:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2599:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10076:   322	**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10078:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10494:   714	// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10740:   293	> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10806:   948	**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10891:    78	- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10929:   255	└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11109:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19:-   **Art 0.2 Tape Canonical 公理**: The design upholds this axiom. The `Sequencer::apply_one` pseudocode in the inherited `CO1.7` spec (§ 3) and the shipped `sequencer.rs` (lines 310-315) show that the pure transition function is called *before* any commit action. If it returns an `Err`, the function returns early, and no `LedgerEntry` is committed. This ensures rejected transactions do not advance the ledger state, which holds for all 7 transition bodies as they are all routed through `dispatch_transition`. The `LedgerEntry.system_signature` attests to the sequencer-stamped semantics of the *accepted* transition, fulfilling the attestation requirement.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11501:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6670:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15195:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:101:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15203:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:198:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17071:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15:- **Art 0.4 Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩ version-controlled**: The implementation explicitly does NOT mutate `head_t`, leaving it at `QState::default` (`sequencer.rs:356-361`). The CO1.7 spec v1.2 § 3 explicitly defers this mutation to CO1.7.5+ wiring (R2-K3 closure). While this is a temporary deviation from the full constitutional vision of Art 0.4, the implementation correctly adheres to the audited spec. The deviation is documented, scoped, and has a planned resolution. It is an acceptable interim state, not an implementation defect. **PASS**.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17781:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:101:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17788:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:198:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:18144:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:10929:   255	└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:18162:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11109:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19:-   **Art 0.2 Tape Canonical 公理**: The design upholds this axiom. The `Sequencer::apply_one` pseudocode in the inherited `CO1.7` spec (§ 3) and the shipped `sequencer.rs` (lines 310-315) show that the pure transition function is called *before* any commit action. If it returns an `Err`, the function returns early, and no `LedgerEntry` is committed. This ensures rejected transactions do not advance the ledger state, which holds for all 7 transition bodies as they are all routed through `dispatch_transition`. The `LedgerEntry.system_signature` attests to the sequencer-stamped semantics of the *accepted* transition, fulfilling the attestation requirement.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19002:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13656:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19010:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13664:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:305:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19058:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13712:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:740:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19078:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13732:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19079:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13733:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1047:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19080:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13734:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1049:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19119:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13773:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19147:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13801:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4807://! deferred to CO1.7.5 (NotYetImplemented stubs in `src/state/sequencer.rs`).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19212:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13866:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8145:   101	In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19220:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13874:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8242:   198	**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.
