handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19705:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15195:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:101:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19713:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15203:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:198:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20556:Atomicity is mostly real for the Git2 path, but the spec overclaims. After `writer_w.commit(&entry)?`, `AtomicU64::store`, `*q_w = q_next`, and field assignments are not fallible (`src/state/sequencer.rs:362-372`). However, `head_commit_oid_hex()` is still a trait dispatch after commit, and the default-`None` path intentionally leaves `head_t` stale (`spec:112-144`). So “`q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` advance atomically” is only true for a Git2 writer that overrides and returns `Some`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20560:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20561:Combined ceremony is permissible, but the spec overstates the protocol. STEP_B Phase 0 asks auditors for the “minimum sufficient version” (`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:9-17`); it does not bind ceremony scoping by itself. A combined A/B unit is still reasonable because `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` are coupled.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20565:- `Kernel` derives `Debug, Serialize, Deserialize` (`src/kernel.rs:18`). Adding `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` as specified (`spec:154-157`) will not compile without `serde(skip)` and Debug handling; `Sequencer` has no derives at `src/state/sequencer.rs:190-207`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20594:The “RejectedAttemptSummary side channel” is not real enough to justify minimal payloads. A type exists at `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:151-158`, but it is not the STATE shape (`STATE:192-214`) and Sequencer rejection currently only logs and skips (`src/state/sequencer.rs:252-266`). No rejected-summary stamping path is wired.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20606:- P5-P12 are materially present (`spec:359-366`, `CLAUDE.md:14`, `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`). P3 is stale wording: it references “§ 6 ack #8” but §6 now has 6 items.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20631:3. Fix D3 wiring spec to compile against actual `TuringBus`/`Kernel` derives, or move Sequencer ownership to a runtime layer with a precise STEP_B target.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20650:Atomicity is mostly real for the Git2 path, but the spec overclaims. After `writer_w.commit(&entry)?`, `AtomicU64::store`, `*q_w = q_next`, and field assignments are not fallible (`src/state/sequencer.rs:362-372`). However, `head_commit_oid_hex()` is still a trait dispatch after commit, and the default-`None` path intentionally leaves `head_t` stale (`spec:112-144`). So “`q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` advance atomically” is only true for a Git2 writer that overrides and returns `Some`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:20654:## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B

A combined A/B unit is still reasonable because `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` are coupled.

handover/audits/2026-04-29_external_audit.md:1271:**Files**: `ROADMAP_9_PHASE...`, `TB-1_recharter...`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`, new `rejection_evidence.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V12_REAUDIT_2026-04-27.md:19:| Q5 STEP_B canonical serialization / ABI / fixtures | Prior Q5 required canonical bytes for tx, `QState`, `SignalBundle`, `TransitionError`, runner ABI, golden fixtures, deterministic fuzz seed, and byte-for-byte success/rejection output comparison. | v1.2 adds §2.5 bincode v2 canonical serialization and a small fixture requirement; full corpus is deferred. | PARTIAL | Prior: `handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:96-103`, reiterated at `:169-173`. v1.2 defines bincode config at `handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:280-300`, requires one golden tx per tx type at `:302-305`, but explicitly defers full golden fixtures and fuzz seed at `:309`. It still does not specify the full runner ABI or byte outputs for `QState`, `SignalBundle`, and `TransitionError`. Concrete failure mode: one branch can implement the prose bullets (`u32` string length, `u8` enum tag) while another follows the shown `bincode::serde` config; without golden bytes, the spec does not arbitrate. `BTreeMap` order is deterministic, but "lexicographic byte order" is only guaranteed if each key type's `Ord` matches its canonical byte encoding. |
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V12_REAUDIT_2026-04-27.md:30:5. STEP_B serialization is improved but still not mechanically pinned: the prose byte rules, shown bincode config, missing runner ABI, and deferred full fixtures leave room for byte-level divergence (`handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:280-309`).
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V12_REAUDIT_2026-04-27.md:36:v1.2 closes several concrete holes: stake return, named bounty/stake/finalize/window invariants, hidden-input classification, integer royalty rounding, and the main false-challenge default. It does not close the original NO-GO fully. The remaining blocker is not effort level; it is spec ambiguity that two STEP_B branches can still encode differently: legacy economic tx disposition is still missing, STEP_B fixtures/ABI are incomplete, and new lifecycle patches introduce contradictions around task expiry, agent init, challenge-window reuse, and finalize order.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V12_REAUDIT_2026-04-27.md:49:2. STEP_B is still not byte-pinned: no full runner ABI, no success/rejection output format, no fixtures for `QState` / `SignalBundle` / `TransitionError`, and full golden/fuzz fixtures are explicitly deferred.

handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_FINAL_2026-04-28.md:77:3. STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for any modifications to `bus.rs` / `kernel.rs` (per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard")
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:176:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:353:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:651:## § 9 STEP_B disposition (K4 corrected typo)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:653:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:660:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3997:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4036:**Pre-implementation gate**: CO1.7 v1 must reach `PASS/PASS` from Codex + Gemini before any `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` or `src/state/sequencer.rs` code is written. Sedimented per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" (Generator ≠ Evaluator) + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4778:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4807:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4809:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5199:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5380:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5433:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5482:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5493:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5544:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5552:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10242:   948	**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10291:   997	**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10302:  1008	This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10451:/bin/bash -lc 'git ls-files src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs src/state/sequencer.rs src/bottom_white/ledger/mod.rs src/bus.rs src/kernel.rs src/wal.rs src/wallet.rs' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10507:## Q-F STEP_B disposition
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10508:No STEP_B ceremony is required for the current skeleton file itself. STEP_B scope is `kernel.rs`, `bus.rs`, `wallet.rs` per CLAUDE and protocol ([CLAUDE.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/CLAUDE.md:14), [STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3)). handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11120:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11149:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11151:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:5:Core runtime correctness looks good: the WorkTx arm is pure, the rejection path preserves K1/no-state-advance discipline, the six r2 P0 compile-shape issues are addressed, `cargo check --workspace`, `cargo test --test tb_2_runtime_boundary`, `cargo test --lib state::sequencer`, and `cargo test --workspace` all passed locally. However, I do not clear merge because the diff exceeds the exact preflight surface in public API visibility and misses one literal STEP_B red-line annotation requirement. These are not VETO-class runtime breaks, but they are before-merge scope/constitutional hygiene fixes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:9:**Q1 — CHALLENGE.** Functionally, the implementation tracks the section-3 list: `SubmissionEnvelope` exists at `src/state/sequencer.rs:302`, `queue_tx` now carries it at `src/state/sequencer.rs:434`, `rejection_writer` is present at `src/state/sequencer.rs:444`, the WorkTx arm is filled at `src/state/sequencer.rs:159`, `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` and `worktx_canonical_hash` are at `src/state/sequencer.rs:57` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:65`, rejection helpers are at `src/state/sequencer.rs:107` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:129`, and the rejection path is at `src/state/sequencer.rs:581`. The 544-line `sequencer.rs` delta is large but mostly tests and trace comments, and the preflight itself declares these targets at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:76` and `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:422`. The challenge is public surface: the preflight snippet showed `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` as `pub(crate)` and `worktx_canonical_hash` as private at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:203`, but implementation exposes them as `pub` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:57` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:65` for integration tests (`tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:34`). That is small, but it is extra exported API.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:11:**Q2 — PASS.** All six r2 P0s are honored. P0-1: the field is exactly `Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:444`, matching `append_rejected(&mut self)` at `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`. P0-2: `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` exists at `src/state/sequencer.rs:100`, and the rejection path uses `submitter_id().unwrap_or_else(...)` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:634`. P0-3: stake uses `micro_units()` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:185`, and the bridge uses `.escrows_t.0.contains_key` / `.task_markets_t.0.contains_key` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:198`; the newtypes are tuple wrappers at `src/state/q_state.rs:161` and `src/state/q_state.rs:224`. P0-4: `StaleParent` is reused at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:720`, only `EscrowMissing` and `MonetaryInvariantViolation` are added at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:788` and `src/state/typed_tx.rs:792`, and Display has exactly those new arms at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:825`. P0-5: integration tests use constructor injection and retained `Arc<RwLock<...>>` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:142` and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:154`; no `rejection_writer_for_test` accessor was found. P0-6: rejection CAS puts take a write lock and call the 5-arg `put` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:597` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:615`, matching the signature at `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:163`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:13:**Q3 — PASS.** The `dispatch_transition` WorkTx arm is pure in the relevant sense. It reads `q`, checks parent/predicates/stake/escrow at `src/state/sequencer.rs:164`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:170`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:177`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:185`, and `src/state/sequencer.rs:197`, calls invariant functions at `src/state/sequencer.rs:213` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:229`, clones local `q_next` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:222`, mutates only that clone's `state_root_t` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:223`, and returns at `src/state/sequencer.rs:236`. There is no CAS, ledger writer, rejection writer, lock acquisition, or external mutation in `src/state/sequencer.rs:159` through `src/state/sequencer.rs:237`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:15:**Q4 — PASS.** The rejection path is disciplined. `dispatch_transition` is called once at `src/state/sequencer.rs:581`; on `Err`, the branch appends L4.E using `submit_id` from the envelope at `src/state/sequencer.rs:643`, then immediately returns `ApplyError::Transition` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:657`. It does not write `q` or `ledger_writer`; those locks are only acquired on the accepted path at `src/state/sequencer.rs:724` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:726`. It does not advance accepted `logical_t`: `rejection_logical_t` is only a snapshot for CAS metadata at `src/state/sequencer.rs:595`, while the only `next_logical_t.store(...)` is accepted-path-only at `src/state/sequencer.rs:732`. CAS writes on rejection are orphan-tolerant per preflight §3.6 at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:337`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:17:**Q5 — PASS.** The 16-test battery is present and green. U1/U2/U3 are in `src/state/sequencer.rs` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1001`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:961`, and `src/state/sequencer.rs:1060`; I1-I13 are in `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:418`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:446`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:188`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:220`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:255`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:285`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:314`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:371`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:510`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:555`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:585`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:615`, and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:645`. I3-I6 assert both `submit_id` and `rejection_class` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:211`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:243`, `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:277`, and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:306`. I7 asserts state root, ledger root, logical counter, and 4 L4.E rows at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:356`. I8 serializes both the raw record and public view and checks `raw_diagnostic_cid` absence at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:400` and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:410`. I12 asserts zero L4.E delta at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:620` and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:634`. I13 uses `replay_full_transition` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:395`) over entries collected from `LedgerWriter::read_at` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:693`, not a `q_snapshot()` shortcut; `q_snapshot()` is only the live comparison target at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:682`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:19:**Q6 — PASS.** `assert_total_ctf_conserved` appears in `sequencer.rs` only as an import at `src/state/sequencer.rs:38` and one production call at `src/state/sequencer.rs:229`, and that call passes `&[]` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:232`. This satisfies preflight red line 4 at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:554`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:21:**Q7 — CHALLENGE.** The changed file set matches the expected broad list from `git diff --name-only f9ace5e..HEAD`: `genesis_payload.toml`, smoke evidence, `rules/enforcement.log`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs`, and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs`. There are no diffs to the forbidden `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, or `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`, whose ban is stated at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:557`. The challenge is narrow: `transition_ledger.rs` is test-only, but it is not literally "JUST matches!() change"; it rewrites the explanatory comment at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1260` and the assertion message at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1294`. I do not see a Trojan there, but it exceeds the exact wording of the verification question.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:25:**Q9 — CHALLENGE.** Trojan sweep is mostly clean: no `unsafe ` occurs in `src/state/sequencer.rs`; `Sequencer::run` only changes receiver payload and forwards the envelope to `apply_one` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:514`; accepted-path CAS/sign/commit/Q update remains the existing sequence at `src/state/sequencer.rs:661` through `src/state/sequencer.rs:739`; `AcceptedLedger` appears only in a doc comment at `src/state/sequencer.rs:54`; `TypedTx` still has exactly the seven existing variants at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:608`. The challenge is public API creep: newly exported `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` and `worktx_canonical_hash` appear at `src/state/sequencer.rs:57` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:65`, while preflight §3.4 showed narrower visibility at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:203`. `try_apply_one` is acceptable because the preflight explicitly declared it at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:100`, and `SubmissionEnvelope` public visibility is implied by public receiver/test-driver signatures at `src/state/sequencer.rs:474`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:516`, and `src/state/sequencer.rs:536`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:29:1. `src/state/sequencer.rs:57` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:65` export `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` and `worktx_canonical_hash`, while the preflight snippet specified `pub(crate)` / private helper shape at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:203`. **Remediation: before merge**, either make the helper/const crate-private and adjust `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:34` plus the expected-hash computation at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:533` to use already-public primitives, or explicitly amend the preflight/trace rationale to authorize this public API.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:31:2. `src/state/sequencer.rs:197` does not carry the exact inline deletion-marker required by preflight red line 9 at `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:559`. The surrounding comment at `src/state/sequencer.rs:189` does say TB-3 deletes the bridge, so the semantic intent is visible, but the literal red-line requirement is not satisfied. **Remediation: before merge**, add the required inline `TB-2 P0-B option (a): drop this when task_open_tx lands in TB-3` marker or update the preflight to accept the existing adjacent comment.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:35:No runtime Trojan found. There are no forbidden-file diffs to `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, or `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`; no `unsafe` in `src/state/sequencer.rs`; no production `AcceptedLedger::append_accepted`; no `EscrowVault` call from the WorkTx arm; no new `TypedTx` variants; and `apply_one` accepted-path semantics remain the same CAS/sign/commit/Q update sequence at `src/state/sequencer.rs:661`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE1C_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:37:Scope creep found but bounded: public helper visibility at `src/state/sequencer.rs:57` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:65`, plus test-comment/assertion-message churn in `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1260` and `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1294`. Neither changes runtime behavior, but both should be cleaned or explicitly sanctioned before merge.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:26:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetMintTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1805-1839` performs a balanced transfer. It debits `balances_t[owner]` and credits `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` by the same `amount`. The `assert_total_ctf_conserved` invariant, extended in TB-13 to include `conditional_collateral_t` as a coin holding (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:186-189`), correctly verifies that this is a migration of funds, not a mint or burn. The integration test `sg_13_1_mint_one_coin_yields_one_yes_plus_one_no_total_coin_conserved` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:88`) explicitly confirms this.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:30:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1840-1923` gates execution on the `task_markets_t` state. The `match` statement on `market_state` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) explicitly returns `Err(TransitionError::RedeemBeforeResolution)` for `TaskMarketState::Open` and `TaskMarketState::Expired`. Redemption is only possible for `Finalized` or `Bankrupt` states. This is confirmed by the integration test `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:170`).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:46:**Finding**: No. The `MarketSeedTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1924-1961` has two gates. First, it rejects zero or negative collateral with `InsufficientCollateral` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1933`). Second, it verifies `provider_bal >= seed.collateral_amount` and rejects with `InsufficientBalanceForMint` if the check fails (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1941`). This is confirmed by tests `sg_13_3_market_seed_fails_if_provider_lacks_balance` and `sg_13_4_market_seed_cannot_create_liquidity_without_collateral`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:54:**Finding**: No. The `ShareAmount` struct in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1003` uses `units: u128`, which is non-negative by definition. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1879` performs a pre-subtraction check: `if owned_units < redeem.share_amount.units { return Err(TransitionError::RedeemMoreThanOwned); }`. This gate prevents any subtraction that could lead to an underflow. This is confirmed by `halt_redeem_more_than_owned_rejected` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:327`).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:80:- **Code**: The `CompleteSetMintTx` accept arm (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1805-1839`) does not check the `task_markets_t` state for the given `event_id`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:94:**Action Item**: Add a state check in the `CompleteSetMintTx` and `MarketSeedTx` dispatch arms in `src/state/sequencer.rs` to reject transactions if `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state != TaskMarketState::Open`. After this fix is implemented and verified by a new integration test, the ship can proceed.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2960:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2989:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2991:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3381:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3562:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3615:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3664:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3675:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3726:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3734:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3832:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4009:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)

It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_5_PHASE0_R3_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:24:The `record_rejection` helper is correctly factored from the current `apply_one` dispatch rejection path: current source snapshots Q, enters dispatch at `src/state/sequencer.rs:939-953`, builds CAS payload and diagnostic CIDs at `src/state/sequencer.rs:956-994`, falls back system-submitters to `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:996-1003`, appends L4.E at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1005-1020`, and returns `ApplyError::Transition` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1022-1024`. The amended preflight helper preserves that shape (`handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:401-445`), and the stage 1.5 failure path calls `self.record_rejection(submit_id, &q_snapshot, &tx, &transition_err)?` before returning `ApplyError::Transition` (`handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:448-461`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_5_PHASE0_R3_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:28:The required tests are now specified: U28/I66 assert L4.E append behavior, and the per-variant zero-signature coverage names for FinalizeReward, TaskExpire, and TerminalSummary are listed (`handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:506-512`). `src/state/sequencer.rs` remains unchanged at the genesis-pinned hash `783e2291c56871a028b860a6fe323d3adc5c00c0e82626cbb05dd40928196bfb` (`genesis_payload.toml:228`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_5_PHASE0_R3_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:36:The blocking issue is a remaining charter table contradiction on `ChallengeStatus`. Preflight §2 correctly says `typed_tx.rs` gets `ChallengeResolution` but not `ChallengeStatus`, and `q_state.rs` is the single definition site imported by `sequencer.rs` (`handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:39-51`). Preflight §6.2 also binds `ChallengeStatus` to `src/state/q_state.rs` only and says `sequencer.rs` imports it via `use crate::state::q_state::ChallengeStatus;` (`handover/ai-direct/TB-5_RSP3_RESOLUTION_GATE_2026-04-30.md:659-685`). However charter §5.2 still attributes `ChallengeStatus` to `src/state/typed_tx.rs` and does not list the enum in the `q_state.rs` row (`handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:297-306`). This fails the round-3 Q4 verification item that both file-touch tables attribute `ChallengeStatus` to `q_state.rs`, not `typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_5_PHASE0_R3_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:56:Q2 PASS, Q4 CHALLENGE, Q6 CHALLENGE, Q7 PASS. There is no VETO: the remaining failures are document consistency defects in the Atom 1.5 amendments, not source-code regressions. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4257:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7357:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4272:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7590:The helper shape is logically correct, but not testable as specified. § 1.1 declares it `pub(crate)` ([spec:78](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:78)), while § 3.3 is a flat integration test calling `turingosv4::state::sequencer::advance_head_t` ([spec:295](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:295), [spec:337](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:337)). Integration tests cannot access `pub(crate)` items. Fix: make the helper `pub` with the required TRACE doc, or move the test into `src/state/sequencer.rs`; MF5’s flat integration-test choice points to `pub`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4274:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7616:MF8 PASS: stale comment cites match current source ([sequencer.rs:180-184](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:180), [sequencer.rs:359-361](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:359)).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5194:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3822:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6239:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:66:| **TFR — TuringOS Foundation Refactor** | Path B real git substrate per Art. 0.4 user decision | 🚀 **LAUNCHED 2026-04-26** | 7-sprint plan (S0–S6) ~7-10 wk; ~50 atoms; STEP_B per atom on bus/kernel/wal/ledger; dual external audit per sprint exit + per STEP_B atom; gix (pure Rust) substrate; per-cell runtime git repo; Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩ literally. Plan: `handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md`. PREREG arc extension proposed (+70d cap; § 9 Proposal A). Pending: user Q1-Q10 decisions + S0 exit dual audit PASS/PASS. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6949:./handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:460:**STEP_B_PROTOCOL files touched in S2**: `src/wal.rs` (S2.3), `src/ledger.rs` (S2.1, S2.2). Per-atom dual-audit on these two atoms specifically.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:6968:./handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:566:Math: ~50 atoms across the arc. STEP_B atoms = ~25. Per-atom external audits = ~25; per-sprint exit audits = 7. Total external audit invocations ≈ 32, at ~30min each = 16 wall-hours of audit-runtime, plus fix-cycle latency. Budget envelope per audit-cycle is ~$8–15 USD at our backbone pricing → ~$300–500 total external audit spend across TFR. Within PREREG § 11 USD-500 cap, but tight — this is one of the open questions in § 10.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7023:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:5:**Principle**: real data decides, not opinion. Parallel branch lets us test change empirically before merging.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7024:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:14:- What's the *minimum sufficient* version? (avoid scope creep)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7025:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:36:- Run `cargo test` on experiment branch → must be green.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7026:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:42:- Are tests sufficient?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7027:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:59:- **Sample size justification**: binomial approximation — a 3-solve difference (6 pp on N=50) is ~2σ above null; 6-solve difference (12 pp) is ~4σ. Pre-register **strict-win threshold = ΔSolveRate ≥ 3** (consistent with v3.1 decision rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7028:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:61:- **Decision rule**:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7029:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:87:External auditors see **data only** (no researcher interpretation). They apply the pre-registered decision rule and return PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7030:./handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:138:- Phase 0: necessity audit — agents converge on same hallucination; recent_errors broadcast currently per-author-only. Is the fix needed, or is v3.2 chat-model test a cheaper alternative?

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7808:./handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:505:STEP_B Branch B re-derivation performed in a separate session against `5ce01b1` (Branch A). Empirical finding: spec § 2.1 was precise enough to converge two independent derivers on **byte-identical executable Rust** (imports, struct field, constructor delegate, forwarder match logic) but **NOT** on literal full-file byte-identity — divergences appeared in (a) doc-comment prose paraphrase, (b) `with_sequencer`/`submit_typed_tx` parameter line-wrapping, and (c) `submit_typed_tx` method placement order within `impl TuringBus`. Pure `cargo fmt` normalization on both branches did NOT reduce divergence to zero (it preserves valid pre-existing wrapping + comment text + method ordering).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7813:./handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:541:2. ✅ ~~CO1.7-extra-impl Branch A~~ — landed at `5ce01b1` (D2 advance_head_t helper + apply_one stage 9 patch + required trait method + 2 impl declarations + TuringBus single-file STEP_B Branch A + 3 substrate-independent tests + stale-comment update at sequencer.rs:178-184/:357-361). ~255 LoC actuals; cargo test 239/0/1.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7998:./handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md:28:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/evidence/tb_2_phase1_smoke_2026-04-30/README.md:40:- **PASS — pipeline-liveness**: evaluator built, ran end-to-end, emitted a v2.0 PPUT row, exited cleanly. Atom 2–6 changes to `src/state/sequencer.rs` + `src/state/typed_tx.rs` + `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` did NOT break the experiment harness.
handover/audits/run_gemini_spec_freeze_audit.py:31:Q1. **Spec completeness for ship gate**: is v1.1 truly ready as the BINDING contract for STEP_B branch A vs branch B comparison? Any concerns vs your prior CHALLENGE?
handover/audits/run_gemini_spec_freeze_audit.py:59:Q8. **STEP_B feasibility prediction**: bus.rs + kernel.rs are ~870 LOC of intertwined state. Spec v1.1 + STEP_B parallel branches would have Claude-A and Codex-B both generating ~800-1000 LOC each. Realistic prediction: how many spec ambiguities will surface during STEP_B? What's the audit cost of resolving them?
handover/evidence/tb_10_lean_market_mvp_smoke_2026-05-02/README.md:88:- ❌ NO new dispatch arm in `sequencer.rs`
handover/evidence/tb_10_lean_market_mvp_smoke_2026-05-02/README.md:245:Empirical finding (charter §2): the kernel currently does NOT verify TaskOpen/EscrowLock Ed25519 signatures (no `verify_agent_signature` call in those dispatch arms; per `src/state/sequencer.rs:1054 + 1095`). Existing evaluator code submits zero-signature variants and the kernel accepts.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:24:    *   **Verification**: Spec § 2.5 makes a critical distinction: the **serialization RULE is frozen** in v1.4 (bincode v2, big-endian, etc.), while the full test fixture **corpus is deferred**. This is an acceptable and well-managed scoping decision. It is not an unresolved ambiguity. It provides STEP_B implementers with a concrete, machine-checkable rule for producing byte-identical digests, which is the necessary condition for cross-branch signature compatibility. The deferral of the full test corpus is a downstream implementation task, not a spec-level blocker.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:42:The specification is implementable, and all blocking issues from prior audit rounds have been resolved. The v1.4 spec provides typed schemas, deterministic pseudocode, a frozen serialization rule, and a comprehensive set of named invariants. This forms a sufficient and binding contract for the two STEP_B implementation branches (A/B) to begin work on atoms CO1.1.4 and CO1.1.5.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:44:## Q5 STEP_B deferral risk
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:49:*   **Role of Fixtures**: The full fixture corpus is for *conformance testing* (i.e., ensuring an implementation correctly handles a wide range of edge cases), not for *defining the canonical format*. The spec itself defines the format. Therefore, the fixtures are not load-bearing for the STEP_B comparison metric of "do both branches produce compatible, signed objects?". The spec alone is sufficient for that.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:53:The STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC v1.4 has successfully closed all outstanding issues. The process of iterative audit and patching has produced a rigorous, deterministic, and implementable specification. The remaining deferrals are explicitly acknowledged and appropriately scoped as downstream implementation tasks, not spec-level ambiguities. The document is certified ready for the CO P1 STEP_B launch.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:57:None. The immediate priority for the implementation teams should be the creation of the initial shared fixture(s) and the conformance test harness, as specified in the deferral acknowledgment (§ 2.5), to enable the mechanical comparison of the STEP_B branches as early as possible. This is now an implementation dependency, not a spec-level concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:3363:**Protocol for restricted-file changes**: `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` (necessity audit → parallel branch → A/B statistical test → merge on empirical win only). Do NOT directly edit restricted files even with authorization; always A/B test.
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:3367:| `recent_rejections` optional global scope | `src/bus.rs` | F-2026-04-15-02 Art. II.1 broadcast | **Human confirm + Step-B** | STEP_B_PROTOCOL |
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:3369:| WAL emission | `src/kernel.rs` or bus.rs | F-2026-04-15-03 | **Human confirm + Step-B** | STEP_B_PROTOCOL |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:3:**CHALLENGE, conviction 5/5.** I verified repository HEAD as `3f06d512983a2bcb5ebc103b203cdaa7412b6362` with `git rev-parse HEAD`. The preflight is directionally right that TB-2 is necessary: `dispatch_transition` is still all-`NotYetImplemented`, `apply_one` returns before any rejection-evidence write, and `submit_id` is not carried into `apply_one` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:53`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:60`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:339`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:346`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:292`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`). However, Phase-1 should not start from this preflight unchanged because it under-specifies required L4.E writer ownership, assumes a clean `task_id` escrow lookup that the current `QState` shape does not provide, and proposes tests that are not all expressible against the current public/integration-test API surface (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:47`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:7:**Q1 [PASS-with-caveat].** The literal restricted set is exactly `src/{kernel,bus}.rs` plus `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` in `CLAUDE.md` and `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` in STEP_B (`CLAUDE.md:14`, `handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`). `src/state/sequencer.rs` is not on that literal path list. STEP_B itself adds the institutional trigger: "Also applicable to any proposal that touches \"institution\" per C-031" (`handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`). C-031 does not explicitly mention STEP_B or path expansion; its relevant authority is institutional priority: "先确认制度 (规则/约束/结构) 正确，再调参数" and "`Build right, then tune`" (`cases/C-031_institution_over_tuning.yaml:16`, `cases/C-031_institution_over_tuning.yaml:18`). Verdict: the preflight applicability argument is correct through STEP_B line 3, but C-031 alone is only policy support, not an explicit path-rule authorization.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:9:**Q2 [PASS-with-minor-overstatement].** Section 2's three broken-behavior claims are true at requested HEAD, although the preflight's "HEAD `459c747`" label is stale relative to verified HEAD `3f06d51` (`handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:42`). For (a), every match arm returns `Err(TransitionError::NotYetImplemented)`: Work through TerminalSummary are lines `54` through `60` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:53`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:60`). For (b), `apply_one` dispatches and uses `?`, so any transition error returns before CAS/sign/commit (`src/state/sequencer.rs:339`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:346`); the `log::debug!` happens in `run`, not inside `apply_one` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:308`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:313`). For (c), the queue is `Sender<TypedTx>`, `submit()` allocates `submit_id`, and `try_send` sends only `tx`; `apply_one` receives only `TypedTx` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:236`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:292`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`). The only overstatement is wording: "`apply_one` ... log::debug!s" should be "`run` log::debug!s after `apply_one` returns."
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:11:**Q3 [CHALLENGE].** The accepted path is already on `transition_ledger`/`LedgerWriter`: `apply_one` canonical-encodes the `TypedTx`, CAS-puts it, builds `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, signs it, folds `ledger_root`, commits via `writer_w.commit(&entry)`, stores `next_logical_t`, writes `q_next`, and sets `q_w.ledger_root_t` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:377`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:386`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:423`). The WorkTx shape mostly supports the validation inputs: it has `task_id`, `parent_state_root`, `agent_id`, `predicate_results`, and `stake` (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:223`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:235`), and `TypedTx` has `tx_kind()` plus `HasSubmitter::submitter_id()` rather than a `submitter_id` field (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:620`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:631`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:641`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:698`). The cascade risk is real: `EconomicState` exposes `escrows_t` and `task_markets_t`, but both are keyed by `TxId`, while `WorkTx.task_id` is a `TaskId` (`src/state/q_state.rs:143`, `src/state/q_state.rs:149`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:33`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:35`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:225`). A task-keyed `EscrowVault` exists, but it is separate from `QState` and explicitly "distinct from `state::q_state::EscrowEntry`" (`src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:15`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:51`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:53`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:146`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:168`). `assert_total_ctf_conserved(..., &[])` is callable, but non-empty exemptions short-circuit (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:167`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:179`). `RejectionEvidenceWriter::append_rejected` has the named fields and is callable (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:268`), but `Sequencer` currently has no rejection writer field or accessor (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`). Missing method/field finding: the preflight must specify how L4.E writer ownership and task-id-to-economic-index lookup work before Phase-1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:13:**Q4 [PASS].** The two-L4-spines concern is technically real as a future-proofing fence, not a current runtime drift. `AcceptedLedger` is an RSP-0 wrapper with an in-memory `Vec` and `current_state_root`, and its own docs say `SystemSignature` attachment and real `Git2LedgerWriter` commit chain are out of scope/deferred (`src/economy/ledger.rs:28`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:32`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:159`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:162`). Its `AcceptedEntry` has seven fields and no system signature (`src/economy/ledger.rs:55`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:72`), while canonical `LedgerEntry` carries `system_signature` and `Git2LedgerWriter` commits one entry per git commit on `refs/transitions/main` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:68`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:92`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:608`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:629`). Current runtime `apply_one` uses `transition_ledger` and not `AcceptedLedger` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:27`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:30`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:423`). Observed `append_accepted` call sites are definition/tests and TB-1 integration tests, not runtime spine calls (`src/economy/ledger.rs:189`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:420`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:149`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:151`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:576`). Verdict: red line is valid future-proofing; it is not correcting an existing `apply_one` drift.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:15:**Q5 [CHALLENGE].** No `TypedTx` variant carries a `submit_id` field today. The current u64-shaped fields are `timestamp_logical`, `last_logical_t`, and `total_attempts`, not submit IDs (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:234`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:247`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:266`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:277`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:309`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:323`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:347`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:349`). Embedding `submit_id` inside `TypedTx` would force a typed ABI change across the enum variants (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:607`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:616`) and would pollute canonical transaction bytes. A tuple `(u64, TypedTx)` would change the same channel/run/apply_one API surface as a named envelope: `queue_tx`, `new()`'s receiver, `run()`, `submit()`, and `apply_one()` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:236`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:270`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:306`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:291`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`). Hidden cost is not cloning: the current code already moves `TypedTx` into `try_send`, and an envelope/tuple would move it likewise (`src/state/sequencer.rs:291`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`). Hidden cost is API/test churn and concurrency semantics: `submit_id` is allocated before `try_send`, so under multiple producers a lower ID can be allocated before a higher ID but arrive later; HYPOTHESIS: tests must assert "receipt ID is preserved in the envelope" rather than "queue order is submit_id order" (`src/state/sequencer.rs:292`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:308`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:19:**Q7 [CHALLENGE].** The 12-test battery is not fully expressible as written against current surfaces. Tests 1-2 need `SubmissionEnvelope` and/or `apply_one(envelope)`, but `apply_one` is `pub(crate)` and an integration test under `tests/` cannot call it (`src/state/sequencer.rs:332`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:130`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:131`). Tests 3-5 need WorkTx predicate/stake/task fixtures, which are structurally constructible from `WorkTx` fields (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:223`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:235`), but L4.E row counting/matching needs a runtime-connected `RejectionEvidenceWriter`, which `Sequencer` does not own today (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:237`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:245`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:327`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:340`). Test 6 is blocked as written: the preflight's "supply-delta-injection" fixture is not a WorkTx field, and WorkTx carries no requested post-state/economic delta (`handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:140`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:158`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:223`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:235`). Tests 7-8 can observe `next_logical_t_peek()` and `q_snapshot()`, but only after the runtime rejection path and L4.E writer are wired (`src/state/sequencer.rs:430`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:443`). Tests 9-11 can observe `state_root_t`, `ledger_root_t`, and `next_logical_t`, and `QState` exposes those fields (`src/state/q_state.rs:313`, `src/state/q_state.rs:317`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:430`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:443`); however, the exact interim hash is not implemented anywhere yet and the proposed domain string appears only in planning docs (`handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:148`, `handover/tracer_bullets/TB-2_charter_2026-04-30.md:56`). Test 12 again requires a runtime-connected L4.E writer (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`). The preflight should either move some tests into `sequencer.rs` unit tests, expose carefully scoped APIs, or revise the battery.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:21:**Q8 [CHALLENGE].** Adversarial sweep found four missed risks. First, rejection path atomicity is under-specified: current accepted path already writes CAS before sign/commit and can leave orphan CAS objects on later failure, while the proposed rejection path adds CAS writes plus L4.E append (`src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:363`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:417`); current `append_rejected` is infallible and in-memory, so the immediate rollback risk is limited, but persistence semantics are deferred (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:30`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:34`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:268`). Second, multi-producer submit order is not specified: `fetch_add` occurs before `try_send`, and `run` processes receiver arrival order (`src/state/sequencer.rs:292`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:308`); HYPOTHESIS: submit ID monotonicity must not be used as an arrival-order guarantee under contention. Third, replay determinism requires all accept-side effects to be represented in L4/QState because `replay_full_transition` re-fetches CAS payloads, re-runs `dispatch_transition`, checks `q_next.state_root_t`, and advances `q` from `q_next` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:371`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:389`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:442`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:486`); apply_one currently ignores `_signals`, so any TB-2 signal side channel would be UNVERIFIED for replay unless ledgered or declared out of scope (`src/state/sequencer.rs:341`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:346`). Fourth, the proposed `turingosv4.worktx.accept.v1` state-root domain is not registered in source or genesis; observed genesis anchors constitution and registry roots only, while the only existing state-root toy domain is `turingosv4.l4_state_root.v1` in `AcceptedLedger` (`genesis_payload.toml:118`, `genesis_payload.toml:127`, `genesis_payload.toml:133`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:350`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:357`). This is not a charter-killer, but it should be made an explicit constant and documented as an interim domain.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:25:1. **Specify and wire runtime L4.E writer ownership before Phase-1.** Evidence: `Sequencer` fields include submit/logical counters, `queue_tx`, CAS, keypair, epoch, `ledger_writer`, registries, and `q`, but no rejection writer (`src/state/sequencer.rs:230`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:247`); `append_rejected` is available but disconnected (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:268`). Remediation: revise the preflight to add the exact `Sequencer` field, constructor parameter, and read-only test accessor for `RejectionEvidenceWriter`, or explicitly route to an existing owner if one exists. Estimated effort: 0.5 day. Scope note: likely `sequencer.rs` plus tests only, unless constructor call sites outside tests appear in Phase-1.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:27:2. **Resolve the `TaskId` versus `TxId` economic lookup mismatch.** Evidence: WorkTx carries `task_id: TaskId`, but `EconomicState.escrows_t` and `task_markets_t` are `BTreeMap<TxId, ...>` (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:225`, `src/state/q_state.rs:161`, `src/state/q_state.rs:224`). A task-keyed `EscrowVault` exists, but it is separate from `QState` and documented as distinct from the L4-state projection (`src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:15`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:53`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:146`, `src/economy/escrow_vault.rs:168`). Remediation: either pre-register a deterministic bridge such as `TxId(tx.task_id.0.clone())`, add a `q_state` helper/index change, or make `EscrowVault` a sequencer dependency. Estimated effort: 0.5 day for bridge, 1 day if `q_state.rs` changes. Scope note: any helper/index change outside `sequencer.rs` is itself a Phase-0 finding against the current "sequencer.rs primary" framing.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:29:3. **Make the acceptance battery compile-expressible.** Evidence: `dispatch_transition` and `apply_one` are `pub(crate)`, but the preflight places tests in `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs` and explicitly wants an `apply_one` signature typecheck test (`src/state/sequencer.rs:47`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:124`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:131`). Evidence: Test 6's `supply-delta-injection` is not a WorkTx field (`handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:158`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:223`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:235`). Remediation: split private-API tests into `sequencer.rs` unit tests, expose only necessary public/test accessors, and rewrite the post-init mint test to use an actually representable transition or mark it as a lower-level monetary-invariant unit. Estimated effort: 0.5-1 day.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:31:4. **Define concrete error and rejection-class mapping.** Evidence: `TransitionError` has `StakeInsufficient`, `TaskNotFound`, and `NotYetImplemented`, but no `EscrowMissing` or `PostInitMint` variants (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:717`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:787`). `RejectionEvidence::RejectionClass` has coarse `PredicateFailed`, `PolicyViolation`, `EscrowMissing`, and `InvariantViolation`, not the exact `StakeRequired`/`PostInitMint` names in the battery (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:56`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:67`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:138`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:140`). Remediation: pre-register `TransitionError -> RejectionClass` mapping and adjust expected test names/classes, or explicitly approve new `TransitionError` variants in `typed_tx.rs`. Estimated effort: 0.25-0.5 day. Scope note: new `TransitionError` variants force an edit outside `sequencer.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:35:1. **Clarify STEP_B institutional authority wording.** STEP_B line 3 is sufficient, but C-031 itself is not an explicit STEP_B path rule (`handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`, `cases/C-031_institution_over_tuning.yaml:16`, `cases/C-031_institution_over_tuning.yaml:18`). Estimated effort: 10 minutes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:37:2. **Justify `SubmissionEnvelope` over tuple `(u64, TypedTx)`.** A tuple changes the same channel/run/apply_one surface, while a named struct is clearer and extensible (`src/state/sequencer.rs:236`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:306`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:332`). Estimated effort: 15 minutes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:39:3. **Add a concurrency note for submit IDs.** `fetch_add` precedes `try_send`, so ID allocation order is not necessarily receiver arrival order under multi-producer scheduling (`src/state/sequencer.rs:292`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:293`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:308`). Estimated effort: 20 minutes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:43:5. **Document orphan-CAS behavior on failed rejection/accepted writes.** CAS `put` is durable before later accepted-path commit steps, and rejection-path CAS plus L4.E append will have similar partial-write considerations (`src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:162`, `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:195`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:363`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:417`). Estimated effort: 30 minutes.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:47:**Revise preflight before Phase-1.** Do not reject the TB-2 charter: the runtime gap is real, the accepted path already has the canonical `transition_ledger` machinery, and the `AcceptedLedger` red line is correct future-proofing (`src/state/sequencer.rs:351`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:423`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:28`, `src/economy/ledger.rs:32`). Do not proceed to Phase-1 until the P0s above are resolved in the preflight: L4.E writer ownership, task/escrow lookup semantics, compile-expressible test plan, and concrete error-class mapping.
src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:10://! deferred to CO1.7.5 (NotYetImplemented stubs in `src/state/sequencer.rs`).

handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:108:No restricted file modified. STEP_B Phase-1 parallel-branch A/B is NOT triggered. D3 production-wire-up Codex impl audit IS still required (round-1 done; round-2 narrow on the v1→v2 diff is the next gate).
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:151:**Codex round-2 ground truth** (verified against `src/state/sequencer.rs`):
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:157:v2.0's shutdown_tx wiring was dead. v2.1 fix: **don't call `Sequencer::run`. Write a runtime-side driver loop in `src/runtime/mod.rs`** that owns the receiver, calls `Sequencer::apply_one` directly, and uses `tokio::select!` on a shutdown channel. Sequencer.rs untouched (no STEP_B trigger).
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:238:`Sequencer.queue_tx` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1093`) is still owned by `Sequencer` and stays alive while `Arc<Sequencer>` is held — but that's fine: `bus.submit_typed_tx` uses it, and after `queue_rx.close()` those sends fail cleanly.
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:285:The 8-arg `append_rejected` signature (`src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:265`) is unchanged. `Sequencer::new` accepts the same `Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>` — no sequencer.rs change.
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:391:Each Atom 1.N reports `cargo test --workspace` count delta per ruling D4. STEP_B not triggered (no restricted file). 24h iteration cap: production-wire-up exception applies (charter § 7.2; Atom 3 must run within 72h of Atom 0 ship).
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:458:2. **Q7+F2 remediation**: does §3.2 `ChaintapeBundle.shutdown()` actually drain queued submissions before driver_handle returns? Does `Sequencer::run` honor a shutdown signal — and if not, what's the actual termination mechanism (drop queue_tx? wait for empty?)? Read `src/state/sequencer.rs:1350+` to verify.
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:463:7. **No-new-finding check**: any hidden bus.rs / sequencer.rs / kernel.rs / wallet.rs touch we missed in v2?
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:480:### §8.2 Sequencer::run shutdown semantics (CLOSED v2.1 — runtime-side wrapper, no STEP_B trigger)
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:482:Codex round-2 verified `Sequencer::run` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1350-1363`) has NO shutdown branch + `Sequencer` owns `queue_tx` (`:1093`) → option (a) "drop queue_tx via Arc count" cannot work because the driver task itself holds `Arc<Sequencer>` keeping the sender alive. Option (b) (modify `Sequencer::run` signature) would trigger STEP_B. **v2.1 picks option (c)**: replace the call to `Sequencer::run` with a runtime-side driver wrapper in `src/runtime/mod.rs` (see §3.2). The wrapper owns the receiver, uses `tokio::select!` on shutdown_rx, and calls `Sequencer::apply_one` (`pub(crate)`; same crate) directly. Sequencer.rs untouched. STEP_B safe. T4 + T10 verify lifecycle correctness.
handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md:494:Per `feedback_step_b_protocol` recommendation, atom-level isolation via `experiment/tb6-chaintape-bootstrap` worktree gives clean rollback even though STEP_B isn't strictly triggered. Disk now permits worktree creation.

handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:563:- **STEP_B_PROTOCOL atoms** (those touching `src/{bus,kernel,wal,ledger}.rs`): external dual-audit per-atom mandatory.
handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:566:Math: ~50 atoms across the arc. STEP_B atoms = ~25. Per-atom external audits = ~25; per-sprint exit audits = 7. Total external audit invocations ≈ 32, at ~30min each = 16 wall-hours of audit-runtime, plus fix-cycle latency. Budget envelope per audit-cycle is ~$8–15 USD at our backbone pricing → ~$300–500 total external audit spend across TFR. Within PREREG § 11 USD-500 cap, but tight — this is one of the open questions in § 10.
