Per memory `feedback_elon_mode_policy` round-cap=2 (now used), v3 takes the auto-execute exception: snippets are line-ref-grounded surgical patches; cargo check inside the STEP_B Phase-1 worktree is the operative verification.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:15:## 0. Why STEP_B applies here
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:17:`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md §0` scope (line 3, verbatim): *"any change to files in CLAUDE.md's restricted list (currently `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`). Also applicable to any proposal that touches 'institution' per C-031."*
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:19:`src/state/sequencer.rs` qualifies under STEP_B line 3's institutional-touch clause:
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:25:C-031 (`cases/C-031_institution_over_tuning.yaml:16,18`) provides policy support for the rule "Build right, then tune" / "先确认制度 (规则/约束/结构) 正确，再调参数" but is *not* itself a path-rule authorization — STEP_B line 3's catch-all is the operative trigger. Per Gemini r1 P1-A, `src/state/sequencer.rs` is also being added to CLAUDE.md's literal restricted list in this revision (see §11 hygiene patches) so future LLM agents do not need to re-derive applicability from case law.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:27:Treat sequencer.rs edits with full STEP_B rigor. Disagreement → conservative verdict (require) per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:37:| `src/state/sequencer.rs` | L4 sequencer + `dispatch_transition` + `apply_one` driver | **YES** (primary). Adds: `SubmissionEnvelope` struct, `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` + `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` consts, `worktx_canonical_hash` / `rejection_class_for` / `public_summary_for` helper fns, `rejection_writer: Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>` field, `pub fn try_apply_one(...)` test driver. Modifies: `Sequencer::new` signature (one new param + receiver type), `Sequencer::run` receiver type, `Sequencer::submit` queue payload, `dispatch_transition` WorkTx arm body, `apply_one` queue type + error path. The two existing `Sequencer::new` call sites at `:483, :627` get the new parameter. |
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:39:| `src/state/q_state.rs` | `QState` snapshot type | **NO** (P1-1 r2 removed v2's "optionally q_state.rs"; constant home pinned to sequencer.rs in §3.4). |
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:49:| `src/state/sequencer.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tb2_runtime_boundary` | TB-2 in-crate unit tests (3 tests) | **NEW** (`pub(crate)` API access for envelope/dispatch_transition signature checks). |
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:57:1. `Sequencer::dispatch_transition` returns `TransitionError::NotYetImplemented` for every `TypedTx` variant — match arms at `src/state/sequencer.rs:54-60`. No real WorkTx ever produces a `q_next`. The accepted L4 spine is therefore unexercised at runtime; the L4.E spine is unreachable from real submissions.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:58:2. `Sequencer::apply_one` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:332`) calls `dispatch_transition(...)?` at `:339, :346` — any transition error propagates via the `?` operator and `apply_one` returns `Err(ApplyError::Transition(e))` without writing CAS, signing, committing, or appending L4.E. The `log::debug!("sequencer apply_one rejected: {e}")` happens in `Sequencer::run` at `:308, :313` AFTER `apply_one` returns the error — i.e. the rejection is logged once at the driver loop, not zero times, but the L4.E primitive is still untouched.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:59:3. `Sequencer::submit` allocates `submit_id` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:292` and returns it in `SubmissionReceipt`, but `try_send` at `:293` carries only `tx`. `apply_one(:332)` receives `TypedTx`, not an envelope. The L4.E identity contract (rejected evidence is `submit_id`-keyed, never `logical_t`-keyed) is unenforceable end-to-end.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:83:// src/state/sequencer.rs (new)
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:91:- `Sequencer.queue_tx: Sender<TypedTx>` → `Sender<SubmissionEnvelope>` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:236`).
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:100:**P1-3 r2 — integration-test driver**: `Sequencer::run(rx)` (`src/state/sequencer.rs:304-:316`) is a `pub async fn` that loops until the receiver closes. There is no single-poll API today, but I3-I13 integration tests need to drive a single submission through `apply_one` to observe its effect synchronously. v3 adds:
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:103:// src/state/sequencer.rs (new pub method — small, intentional public test driver)
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:115:Receiver ownership note: `Sequencer::new` returns `(Sequencer, Receiver<SubmissionEnvelope>)`. Production passes the receiver into `run`; tests retain it and pass to `try_apply_one`. The pre-v3 receiver-on-`Sequencer-self` shape is verified at `src/state/sequencer.rs:271, :304` — receiver is moved into `run` at `:304`, so it's not stored on `Sequencer` itself; v3 exposes the same external receiver back to tests instead.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:119:**Verified shape** (r2 audit: `RejectionEvidenceWriter::append_rejected` is `&mut self` per `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:226, 258`; existing `Sequencer` already wraps mutable shared state in `Arc<RwLock<...>>` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:238, 241`). `Arc<RejectionEvidenceWriter>` would NOT compile. The matching shape is:
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:122:// src/state/sequencer.rs — accurate shape post-v3 (existing fields verified vs
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:159:- **All existing `Sequencer::new(...)` call sites in repo (verified via r2 audit): exactly TWO** — `src/state/sequencer.rs:483` and `:627`. Both are inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod` and need the new parameter passed as `Arc::new(RwLock::new(RejectionEvidenceWriter::default()))`. (`RejectionEvidenceWriter` derives `Default` per its declaration at `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:20`.) No production call site updates are needed because no production code currently constructs a `Sequencer` outside test fixtures.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:201:**Location pinned** (P1-1 r2): `src/state/sequencer.rs` (alongside the other state machinery; `q_state.rs` is data-shape only). Removed the v2 ambiguity ("`sequencer.rs` or `q_state.rs`").
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:204:// src/state/sequencer.rs (new constant — top of file, near other domain constants)
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:308:`SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` is a `pub(crate) const &str = "__system__"` declared near `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` in `src/state/sequencer.rs`. The string content is internal-only; it never crosses the agent boundary (only `public_summary` does, per `RejectedSubmissionRecord.agent_id` doc-comment at `rejection_evidence.rs:89-90`).
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:312:Defined locally in `sequencer.rs` alongside `rejection_class_for`:
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:335:Accepted path stays on the **existing** `transition_ledger` + `LedgerWriter` flow already wired into `apply_one`'s success arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:351, :377, :386, :423` — TB-2 does not introduce a new ledger writer and does not change the accepted-side commit sequence.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:422:**Scope creep guard**: any line of code outside §3.1–§3.7 (production-code §) constitutes a separate atom and must be extracted into TB-3+ unless the auditors explicitly approve it in Phase-1c. The only files touched by §3.1–§3.7 are `src/state/sequencer.rs` (primary — adds `SubmissionEnvelope`, `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1`, `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR`, `worktx_canonical_hash`, `rejection_class_for`, `public_summary_for`, `try_apply_one`, `rejection_writer` field, fills WorkTx arm of `dispatch_transition`, rewrites `apply_one` error path) and `src/state/typed_tx.rs` (TWO new `TransitionError` variants: `EscrowMissing` + `MonetaryInvariantViolation`; corresponding 2 lines in the `Display` impl). `q_state.rs` constant home was rejected per §3.4 P1-1 r2.
handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:434:Implements §3 minimum-sufficient version. Acceptance battery is split between in-crate unit tests (`#[cfg(test)] mod tb2_runtime_boundary` inside `src/state/sequencer.rs` — for `pub(crate)` API checks) and integration tests (`tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs` — for behaviour through `Sequencer::submit`). Total 16 tests across both surfaces (see §5). Each test added in red→green order; commit boundaries respect `feedback_phased_checkpoint` (paired N=20 not applicable — runtime spine is deterministic per submission, no LLM in the loop yet).

Any edit outside this surface fails Phase-1c review.

handover/ai-direct/TB-3_RSP1_FORMAL_TX_SURFACE_2026-04-30.md:63:4. **`WorkTx.stake` admission is event-bound by WP § 18 Inv 5 but not realized at runtime**: TB-2's accepted WorkTx leaves `economic_state_t` unchanged (`src/state/sequencer.rs:217-221` docstring: "TB-2 does not yet move stake/escrow balances; RSP-1 lifecycle is TB-3+"). Solver could submit infinite work_txs each declaring large stake without any balance commitment; the "1 Coin = 1 YES + 1 NO" CTF promise is structurally unredeemed.

handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:309:**Pre-implementation gate**: spec must reach PASS/PASS (or PASS-with-OBS) before any code in `rules/active/R-022*` / `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` / `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` / `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is written. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard". No STEP_B-restricted files touched (kernel.rs / bus.rs / wallet.rs UNTOUCHED).
handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:332:6. **No STEP_B-restricted file touches**: kernel + bus + wallet untouched. Pure-additive at `rules/active/`, `scripts/`, `.git/hooks/`, `tests/`. No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:293:> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:322:**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:714:// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:895:**Total: 27 invariants → 27 tests** (was 22 in v1.1; +5 in v1.2). Every transition test must pass before CO1.1.4 (bus.rs split) starts. STEP_B implementation comparison is "branch X conforms to spec" / "branch Y conforms to spec", not "branch X looks like branch Y".
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:948:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:997:**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check. STEP_B branch A vs branch B both implement the same `is_open()`; cross-branch comparison verifies identical results for all (opens_at, duration_ticks, now) triples.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:1008:This means: STEP_B branch A and branch B may serialize threads differently (depending on OS scheduler), but as long as both branches use atomic logical_t assignment + replay from the SAME logical_t sequence, they produce byte-identical state_roots.
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:1059:6. STEP_B implementation: Claude implements branch A against spec; Codex implements branch B against spec; comparison metric = "spec conformance", not "code similarity"
handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:1067:- A binding contract for STEP_B branch A/B comparison
handover/specs/CO1_8_MATERIALIZER_v1_2026-04-29.md:125:Sub-atoms are NOT independently shippable in v1 — they form a single compile unit (mod.rs re-exports cascade). Decomposition exists for audit-readability and future-refactor predictability. Each sub-atom MAY get its own STEP_B-non-restricted commit during impl phase if size warrants (>300 LoC delta).
handover/specs/CO1_8_MATERIALIZER_v1_2026-04-29.md:219:**Pre-implementation gate**: spec must reach PASS/PASS before any code in `src/bottom_white/materializer/**` is written. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard". No STEP_B-restricted files touched (kernel.rs / bus.rs / wallet.rs UNTOUCHED by v1; only new files under `src/bottom_white/materializer/`).
handover/specs/CO1_8_MATERIALIZER_v1_2026-04-29.md:240:6. **No STEP_B-restricted file touches**. Kernel + bus + wallet untouched. v1 is pure-additive at `src/bottom_white/materializer/**`. No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required.

handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:503:### v1.2.2 (this revision; STEP_B Branch B closure)
handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:505:STEP_B Branch B re-derivation performed in a separate session against `5ce01b1` (Branch A). Empirical finding: spec § 2.1 was precise enough to converge two independent derivers on **byte-identical executable Rust** (imports, struct field, constructor delegate, forwarder match logic) but **NOT** on literal full-file byte-identity — divergences appeared in (a) doc-comment prose paraphrase, (b) `with_sequencer`/`submit_typed_tx` parameter line-wrapping, and (c) `submit_typed_tx` method placement order within `impl TuringBus`. Pure `cargo fmt` normalization on both branches did NOT reduce divergence to zero (it preserves valid pre-existing wrapping + comment text + method ordering).
handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:507:**Resolution**: § 2.2 amended in this revision to define a **3-tier byte-identity** (T1 executable substance REQUIRED; T2 formatting & layout MAY drift; T3 doc-comment prose MAY drift). Branch B re-derivation passed T1 unanimously across all 5 substantive surfaces (imports / field / `new()` body / `with_sequencer` body / `submit_typed_tx` match arms). **STEP_B ceremony for `src/bus.rs` is therefore CLOSED**.
handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:509:The amendment is itself a process-spec refinement (not a mathematical re-statement) and does not require re-audit per the same precedent as v1.2.1's editorial nits — a tiered byte-identity is strictly less permissive than the prior absent definition (which had no formal closure criterion at all besides "identical → merge"; the prior wording silently relied on STEP_B implementers' unspoken judgement that rustfmt-equivalent drift was acceptable).
handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:521:- **MF4** Sequencer placement: TuringBus owns directly (NOT nested through Kernel). Kernel UNTOUCHED. STEP_B becomes single-file ceremony (§ 2.1 + § 2.2).

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-11_charter_2026-05-02.md:133:**Naming reconciliation with existing code** (per ruling annotation §3): the codebase already pre-defines `TerminalSummaryTx` (in `src/state/typed_tx.rs` § 5 line 354) and `TaskExpireTx` (line 329) with `NotYetImplemented` dispatch arms (sequencer.rs:962-963). The architect's `RunExhaustedTx` is **semantically equivalent to TerminalSummaryTx in the failure path** (both anchor a run-level outcome). Resolution: extend `TerminalSummaryTx` additively with `parent_state_root` + `solver_agent: Option<AgentId>` + `evidence_capsule_cid: Option<Cid>` (no production rows on this variant, safe per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`); document the architect-vocabulary alias in code comments. `TaskBankruptcyTx` is genuinely net-new.

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:13:Formalize RSP-1 admission as **two first-class TypedTx variants** on the canonical L4 — `TaskOpenTx` (task metadata registration) and `EscrowLockTx` (sole bounty funding path). **Delete** the TB-2 P0-B option (a) bridge at `src/state/sequencer.rs:205-215`. `WorkTx.stake` stays inline per WP § 14.1 + § 18 Invariant 5; **no `YesStakeTx` variant is introduced**. After TB-3, WorkTx admission is structural (looks up real ChainTape-recorded escrow + checks inline stake against agent solvency), not bridged from a synthetic `TxId(task_id.0)` lookup, and accepted WorkTx commits stake by debiting balances → stakes_t in a single conserved transition.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:44:- **No `YesStakeTx` TypedTx variant is introduced**. Adding one would be ABI-level work that contradicts the WP data-structure shape; doing it would also re-introduce the very same indirection class (synthetic ID matching) that the bridge at `sequencer.rs:205` was an apology for.

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md:395:> After Proof 1 setup, a predicate-passing `WorkTx`(task_id=T, agent=Solver, stake=K) where `balances_t[Solver] >= K` is admitted, advances `state_root_t` to `sha256(WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1 ‖ prev ‖ canonical_hash(work))`, advances `ledger_root_t`, increments `logical_t`, debits `balances_t[Solver]` by K, inserts `stakes_t[work.tx_id] = StakeEntry { amount: K, staker: Solver, task_id: T }`. Each missing precondition (no TaskOpen / no EscrowLock / Solver balance < K / stake==0) routes the WorkTx to L4.E with the appropriate class WITHOUT mutating economic_state. The bridge code at `sequencer.rs:205-215` is deleted in the same commit; `bridge_pattern_does_not_resurrect_in_src` test passes. (Tests I23 + I24 + I25 + I26 + I27 + I28.)

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md:315:## 12. Open questions for Atom 1 STEP_B preflight

handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:15:Charter v1 (`1b60237`) proposed `ChallengeResolveTx` as a system-emitted economic mutator but **failed to specify a live ingress / signature gate**. Codex round-1 audit B2 verified the gap: `Sequencer::submit` accepts bare `TypedTx` from any caller (`src/state/sequencer.rs:863`), and `dispatch_transition` does not verify `system_signature` (entire src/ has zero signature-verification calls; `PinnedSystemPubkeys` is replay-side only). Result: any agent could craft `ChallengeResolveTx{Released}` and refund any challenge bond at any time. **Constitutional-level VETO** (Anti-Oreo: agent ≠ direct state writer).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB-5_charter_2026-04-30.md:114:Existing `Sequencer::submit()` (the bare-TypedTx path at `src/state/sequencer.rs:863`) is **either** narrowed to delegate to `submit_agent_tx` (rejecting system variants pre-dispatch) **or** removed entirely. Charter v2 implementation Atom 2 picks the safer option (narrow + reject); Atom 3 may remove the legacy alias once all internal callers migrate.

handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:10:# 2026-04-30 TB-2 charter (active) — "P1/P3 Runtime Boundary Closure + RSP-1". Goal: real WorkTx traverses Sequencer::dispatch_transition; accepted → canonical L4 (transition_ledger + LedgerWriter, NOT economy::ledger::AcceptedLedger which stays a TB-1 primitive); rejected → L4.E with submit_id; RSP-1 admission via WorkTx.stake>0 + seeded EconomicState escrow. STEP_B-protected target: src/state/sequencer.rs. Forbidden Day-1: ledger I/O inside dispatch_transition; new TypedTx variants; non-empty exempt_tx_kinds at runtime; P5/P6/h_vppu work; AcceptedLedger as production accepted spine. Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-2_charter_2026-04-30.md. STEP_B preflight: handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:12:# 2026-04-30 TB-3 charter v2 + STEP_B Phase-0 preflight v1 — "P3 RSP-1 Formal Tx Surface (WP-canonical)". Goal: 2 new TypedTx variants (TaskOpenTx + EscrowLockTx) on canonical L4; bridge at sequencer.rs:197-215 deleted; WorkTx admission becomes structural (task_markets_t[task_id].total_escrow > 0 + balances_t[agent] >= work.stake); accepted WorkTx commits stake (balances_t debit + stakes_t insert per WP § 14.1 + § 18 Inv 5 lock-on-accept); rejected WorkTx leaves economic_state_t bit-identical (L4.E never mutates economic state per user verdict #14). Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-3_charter_2026-04-30.md. Preflight: handover/ai-direct/TB-3_RSP1_FORMAL_TX_SURFACE_2026-04-30.md.
handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:13:# 2026-04-30 TB-5 SHIPPED — merged at 1bdc55a (--no-ff merge of experiment/tb5-rsp3-resolution-gate into main). Eight atoms (4c3414e Atom 1 STEP_B preflight → 66f559e Atom 1.5 round-2 remediation → b9de549 Atom 1.6 round-3 doc-fix → c415cd2 round-4 self-verification fallback → 42fd45c Atom 2 substrate ingress → 4a33b1a Atom 3 ABI → 9ff8179 Atom 4 emit_system_tx + apply_one stage 1.5 → 06a7fcf Atom 5 ChallengeResolve dispatch arm → c7dfef9 Atom 6 UpheldDeferred + boundary tests → cc72d61 Atom 7 replay + property + anti-drift CI → 2fb4ed9 Atom 8 recursive audit + 真题烟测). **617/617 cargo test --workspace passing** (corrected 2026-05-01 per `handover/audits/SELF_AUDIT_TB_5_SMOKE_TAPE_2026-05-01.md` § 2 — original ship-time figure of 464 was bare `cargo test` count without --workspace; net delta from TB-4 baseline 571 = 46 new TB-5 tests). Production claim adds: "TuringOS runtime kernel structurally enforces Anti-Oreo agent-vs-system ingress separation. Agent ingress (submit_agent_tx) rejects 4 system-emitted variants pre-queue with SubmitError::SystemTxForbiddenOnAgentIngress. System ingress (emit_system_tx) constructs + signs system-emitted TypedTx structs INTERNALLY with the runtime's Ed25519Keypair; defense-in-depth verification at apply_one stage 1.5 re-checks signatures against PinnedSystemPubkeys. ChallengeResolve dispatch arm enforces idempotent single-shot resolution: Released refunds challenger bond (entry preserved); UpheldDeferred is marker-only with bond preserved for TB-6 RSP-3.2 slash routing. CTF conserved across mixed Released + UpheldDeferred sequences; 9-sub-field EconomicState invariant + 5-holding CTF preserved." Audit trail: STEP_B Phase-0 r1+r2+r3 (Codex round-2 4 CHALLENGE → 1.5 amendments; round-3 2 CHALLENGE → 1.6 doc-fix; round-4 Codex agent infra failure → self-verification fallback). Codex-only audit mode per `2026-04-30_TB5_audit_mode_supplement.md` (Gemini strategic-tier MODEL_CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED).
handover/tracer_bullets/TB_LOG.tsv:14:# 2026-05-01 TB-6 ACTIVE — "P2 Agent Runtime: Production ChainTape Wire-up". Architect ruling 2026-05-01 (`handover/directives/2026-05-01_TB6_ARCHITECT_RULING.md`) selected Path A (P2 Agent Runtime / Production ChainTape Wire-up) over Path B (RSP-3.2 Slash) per D1. 7 binding decisions ruled: D1 Path A; D2 chain-backed smoke = HARD requirement from TB-6; D3 hybrid-by-risk audit (TB-6 = production wire-up class → Codex impl + Gemini arch with degraded fallback); D4 `cargo test --workspace` canonical with mandated reporting shape; D5 historical "smoke tape" → "smoke evidence" rename (tape only for ledger chain); D6 5 memory updates approved; D7 NO constitution amendment (this is roadmap/testing-platform gap, not constitutional). Goal: at least one LLM-driven run traverses rtool/context → Agent proposal → WorkTx → Sequencer::apply_one → LedgerWriter::commit → on-disk ChainTape → replay/verify_chain/reconstruct, producing ≥1 accepted L4 + ≥1 rejected L4.E entries with valid system_signature + parent_ledger_root chain + CAS payload linkage + agent audit trail (proposal CIDs only; NO chain-of-thought). 8 atoms per ruling § 3.6: Atom 0 charter+naming cleanup → Atom 1 production runtime repo bootstrap (RuntimeChaintapeConfig; bus.rs + main.rs; STEP_B preflight) → Atom 2 evaluator → Sequencer adapter → Atom 3 chain-backed smoke run (mathd_algebra_107) → Atom 4 verify_chaintape CLI → Atom 5 agent audit trail → Atom 6 fork visibility → Atom 7 audit + ship. Forbidden: SlashTx, NodeMarket/RSP-M, AMM, P6 expansion, MetaTape, public chain, new TypedTx variants, new state-root domains, new monetary cascade, agent chain-of-thought broadcast/persistence, calling pre-TB-6 paper trail "smoke tape"/"chaintape"/"tape", Gemini-degraded-mode label-omission. Charter: handover/tracer_bullets/TB-6_charter_2026-05-01.md. STEP_B preflight (deferred to Atom 1): handover/ai-direct/TB-6_PRODUCTION_CHAINTAPE_BOOTSTRAP_2026-05-01.md.

/bin/bash -lc "test -f handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md && nl -ba handover/ai-direct/STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md | sed -n '1,120p' || true" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Step-B Protocol: Restricted-File Changes via Parallel Branch Experiment
     2	
     3	**Scope**: any change to files in CLAUDE.md's restricted list (currently `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). Also applicable to any proposal that touches "institution" per C-031.
     4	
     5	**Principle**: real data decides, not opinion. Parallel branch lets us test change empirically before merging.
     6	
     7	---
     8	
     9	## Phase 0 — Necessity audit (external, before any code)
    10	
    11	External auditors (**Codex + Gemini**) answer:
    12	- Is the change *necessary*? What observable behavior is broken now?
    13	- Is a less-invasive alternative available? (same effect, no restricted-file touch)
    14	- What's the *minimum sufficient* version? (avoid scope creep)
    15	- What's the failure mode if we don't change?
    16	
    17	**Gate**: if both auditors say "less-invasive alternative exists", take that path instead. If both say "change is necessary", proceed to Phase 1. If they disagree, take the conservative verdict per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`.
    18	
    19	**My role**: write the audit brief; do not pre-advocate. If I agree with the verdict after reading both, proceed. If I disagree, record dissent in notepad §7 before proceeding.
    20	
    21	---
    22	
    23	## Phase 1 — Parallel branch creation
    24	
    25	### 1a. Worktree spawn
    26	```bash
    27	git worktree add .claude/worktrees/stepb-<slug> -b experiment/<slug>
    28	```
    29	
    30	Or via Agent tool with `isolation: worktree` frontmatter — for short experiments (<2h). Agent returns branch name + diff summary.
    31	
    32	### 1b. Implementation in isolation
    33	- Work in the experiment branch only.
    34	- Main branch stays at the last audited-PASS state.
    35	- Add fixture tests covering the change.
    36	- Run `cargo test` on experiment branch → must be green.
    37	- Commit with message `experiment/<slug>: <change>` (not merged).
    38	
    39	### 1c. Implementation audit
    40	External auditors (same two) review the **diff only**:
    41	- Is the change minimal?
    42	- Are tests sufficient?
    43	- Does it introduce new constitutional debt?
    44	- Any risk the diff itself is a Trojan (side-effects beyond scope)?
    45	
    46	**Gate**: both PASS on diff → proceed to Phase 2. Any VETO → abandon branch or revise.
    47	
    48	---
    49	
    50	## Phase 2 — Statistical A/B test (pre-registered)
    51	
    52	### 2a. Pre-register experimental design
    53	Before any batch run, write a locked spec to `handover/ai-direct/AB_<slug>_<date>.md`:
    54	
    55	- **Null hypothesis (H0)**: p(treatment solves) = p(control solves) on frozen sample
    56	- **Primary metric**: SolveRate delta (per `metrics.yaml`)
    57	- **Secondary metrics**: Aggregate_PPUT, mean wall time
    58	- **Sample**: identical to M4 (seed=74677, N=50, fingerprint=796ead6c40351ae9)
    59	- **Sample size justification**: binomial approximation — a 3-solve difference (6 pp on N=50) is ~2σ above null; 6-solve difference (12 pp) is ~4σ. Pre-register **strict-win threshold = ΔSolveRate ≥ 3** (consistent with v3.1 decision rule).
    60	- **Statistical test**: McNemar's test for paired nominal data. Report p-value but do not use as primary (SolveRate delta is).
    61	- **Decision rule**:
    62	  - `ΔSolveRate ≥ 3`: treatment strict win → merge candidate
    63	  - `-1 ≤ Δ ≤ 2`: inconclusive → either expand N (same seed family) or abandon
    64	  - `ΔSolveRate ≤ -1`: treatment regresses → abandon branch
    65	- **Interleaving**: run both conditions on each problem alternately (or in parallel if possible) to neutralize API drift (C-033).
    66	- **Abort gate**: per-condition 20% / 30% (same as v3.1).
    67	
    68	### 2b. Execute on both branches
    69	- **Control branch** (main or last-PASS HEAD): run `run_interleaved.sh` with `TREATMENT_LABEL=control`
    70	- **Treatment branch** (experiment/<slug>): run same script with `TREATMENT_LABEL=treatment`
    71	- **Cost**: 2× the single-arm experiment. Pre-registered budget before spending.
    72	
    73	### 2c. Freeze analyzer
    74	`frozen_analysis.py` for A/B extension:
    75	- Must support `--control <jsonl>` and `--treatment <jsonl>` flags
    76	- Outputs: paired-comparison table, McNemar p, ΔSolveRate, discordant pairs list
    77	- Must be fixture-tested before A/B run (C-012 mandatory freeze).
    78	
    79	---
    80	
    81	## Phase 3 — Verdict + commit path
    82	
    83	### 3a. Read the data
    84	After both branches finish, run `frozen_analysis.py --control ... --treatment ...`.
    85	
    86	### 3b. Audit the verdict (again)
    87	External auditors see **data only** (no researcher interpretation). They apply the pre-registered decision rule and return PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE.
    88	
    89	### 3c. Merge or abandon
    90	- **Treatment win (audits PASS)**: 
    91	  - `git merge experiment/<slug> --no-ff` on main
    92	  - Update notepad §2 with new F-id
    93	  - If new constitutional pattern emerged → write case candidate
    94	- **Treatment lose or inconclusive**:
    95	  - `git branch -D experiment/<slug>` (or archive as tag `archive/<slug>_<date>`)
    96	  - Update notepad §3 (retracted hypotheses) with finding
    97	  - No commit to main; keep last-PASS HEAD
    98	
    99	### 3d. Cleanup
   100	- Remove worktree: `git worktree remove .claude/worktrees/stepb-<slug>`
   101	- If branch archived: `git tag archive/<slug>_<date> experiment/<slug>` then delete branch
   102	
   103	---
   104	
   105	## What this protocol guarantees
   106	
   107	1. **C-010**: external audit at 3 junctures (necessity, diff, verdict). Researcher cannot self-approve a restricted change.
   108	2. **C-012**: pre-registered metrics, frozen sample, fixture-tested analyzer — no post-hoc metric shopping.
   109	3. **C-033**: paired-comparison on same problems cleanly attributes any change to the code diff, not to sample drift.
   110	4. **C-034**: the change is a mechanism edit validated by mechanism-level data, not prompt tuning.
   111	5. **Art. V.2**: pre-registered budget. No scope creep.
   112	
   113	## What this protocol does NOT do
   114	
   115	- **Statistical power** is limited by frozen N=50. Small effects (Δ = 1-2 solves) stay inconclusive. Pre-register a larger N (100? 244?) if effect is expected small.
   116	- **Non-stationary effects** (model drift, API latency changes) across days are not controlled unless both branches run interleaved or within same hour.
   117	- **Multi-batch meta-analysis** is not automated; manual aggregation if we run the same A/B protocol multiple times.
   118	
   119	---
   120

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:32:Net-new for TB-13 (commits to anchor your reads):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:48:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:58:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Does any new TB-13 module file import legacy `prediction_market`? Trace `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` span detector. The fence's authoring-marker rule: only spans whose first non-blank line matches `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13` / `// TB-13 ` / `//! TB-13 ` / `/// TB-13 ` are in scope. Verify the rule cannot be bypassed by writing a TB-13 import OUTSIDE a TB-13-marker span (e.g., adding a `use crate::prediction_market::BinaryMarket` at the top of `src/state/sequencer.rs` without a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be caught). Is the fence robust enough?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:64:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:95:**RQ6 — Forward-fence span detector robustness**: The fence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` only checks lines inside spans with a TB-13 authoring marker. A new TB-13 file that does NOT carry a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be in scope. Is this exploitable? Recommend: should the fence also scan for TB-13-named files (`src/state/conditional_market.rs` etc.) regardless of marker, OR is the marker-discipline sufficient?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:97:**RQ7 — STEP_B protocol compliance**: TB-13 modifies `src/state/sequencer.rs` (restricted file per CLAUDE.md Code Standard). The TB-12 precedent did so via direct edit (additive dispatch arms). TB-13 follows the same pattern. Is this within STEP_B? Or should TB-13 have used parallel-branch A/B?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1152:**Scope**: TB-13 Atoms 0 + 0.5 + 1 + 2 + 3 + 5 SHIPPED to local main; Atom 4 (dashboard §14) DEFERRED to TB-14 (not in architect Part A spec); this document is Atom 6(a). External Codex + Gemini audits pending — Atom 6(b) + 6(c).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1167:| Article                                  | TB-13 invariant                                                                                                | Verification                                                                              |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1170:| Art. I.1 5-step compile loop closure     | Conditional collateral & share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome (CR-13.6: "Price / share state cannot override predicates or challenge outcome"). | TB-13 dispatch arms operate purely on `economic_state_t.{balances_t, conditional_collateral_t, conditional_share_balances_t}`; predicate-evaluation paths (TB-3 Work / TB-4 Verify+Challenge / TB-5 ChallengeResolve / TB-8 FinalizeReward) UNCHANGED. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1173:| Art. V.1.3 Anti-Oreo                     | All 3 TB-13 typed-tx are AGENT-SIGNED (CompleteSetMint / CompleteSetRedeem / MarketSeed). NO new system_tx variant introduced. Provider funds are EXPLICIT in MarketSeedTx (NO automatic seed; CR-13.1 + CR-13.2 forbid ghost / automatic liquidity). | `submit_agent_tx` ingress agent-fall-through arm extended for 3 new variants (verified via reading `src/state/sequencer.rs:1996..2002`). NO `emit_system_tx` arm added for TB-13. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1181:| Property                                  | TB-13 verification                                                                                                                |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1184:| Cross-instance replay equality            | TB-3..TB-12 replay invariants unchanged. TB-13 dispatch arms add to `q_next.economic_state_t.{balances_t, conditional_collateral_t, conditional_share_balances_t}` only — each mutation is field-by-field deterministic. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1193:| Conservation invariant                    | TB-13 enforcement                                                                                                                                                                                |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1195:| **6-holding CTF (post-TB-13)**            | Σ holdings = balances_t + escrows_t + stakes_t + challenge_cases_t + conditional_collateral_t. Atom 3 extends the TB-7R 5-holding sum to 6 by adding `conditional_collateral_t` per architect CR-13.4 ("Locked collateral is Coin holding"). conditional_share_balances_t INTENTIONALLY OMITTED per CR-13.3 + SG-13.2. |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1199:| `assert_no_post_init_mint`                | All 3 TB-13 variants in exhaustive match arm — none create money (mint = balance↔collateral migration; redeem = collateral↔balance migration; seed = balance↔collateral migration). PASS. |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4029:# Codex TB-13 Ship Audit — implementation-paranoid
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4031:**Role**: skeptical adversarial implementer-reviewer for the TB-13 (CompleteSet + MarketSeedTx) ship-gate dual external audit. Independent of Gemini ship audit (parallel, architectural angle).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4037:- Charter: `handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md`
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4044:TB-13 range:       fa36eca..HEAD     (this audit)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4048:Net-new for TB-13 (commits to anchor your reads):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4064:**Q4 (6-holding conservation)**: Does the 6-holding `total_supply_micro` sum hold across all TB-13 typed_tx? Walk `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152..198` `total_supply_micro` — it now includes `conditional_collateral_t.0.values()` after the 5-holding sum. `assert_total_ctf_conserved` is called from each TB-13 dispatch arm with empty exempt list. Test: `sg_13_1`, `sg_13_2`, `halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4074:**Q9 (forward-fence)**: Does any new TB-13 module file import legacy `prediction_market`? Trace `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` span detector. The fence's authoring-marker rule: only spans whose first non-blank line matches `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13` / `// TB-13 ` / `//! TB-13 ` / `/// TB-13 ` are in scope. Verify the rule cannot be bypassed by writing a TB-13 import OUTSIDE a TB-13-marker span (e.g., adding a `use crate::prediction_market::BinaryMarket` at the top of `src/state/sequencer.rs` without a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be caught). Is the fence robust enough?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4080:**RQ2 — CTF conservation**: Walk the math for each of the 3 TB-13 dispatch arms. At CompleteSetMint:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4111:**RQ6 — Forward-fence span detector robustness**: The fence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` only checks lines inside spans with a TB-13 authoring marker. A new TB-13 file that does NOT carry a TB-13 doc-comment marker would NOT be in scope. Is this exploitable? Recommend: should the fence also scan for TB-13-named files (`src/state/conditional_market.rs` etc.) regardless of marker, OR is the marker-discipline sufficient?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4113:**RQ7 — STEP_B protocol compliance**: TB-13 modifies `src/state/sequencer.rs` (restricted file per CLAUDE.md Code Standard). The TB-12 precedent did so via direct edit (additive dispatch arms). TB-13 follows the same pattern. Is this within STEP_B? Or should TB-13 have used parallel-branch A/B?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4147:I’ll audit this as a code review first: read the governing instructions, then trace the TB-13 transaction schemas, sequencer arms, invariants, tests, and smoke evidence against the mandated questions. Because this session is read-only, I’ll verify what I can from the workspace and call out any inability to write the final audit file.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4678:src/state/typed_tx.rs:1098:/// TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3): non-negative share count.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4700:I have enough to issue the audit. I found two blocking defects: negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by the TB-13 mint/seed arms, and the TB-13 “agent-signed” variants are never live-verified at submit/apply time. I’m going to try to write the requested audit file now; this workspace is read-only, so the write may be rejected.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4707:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4709:- TB13-V2 VETO: The TB-13 `AgentSignature` fields are not live-verified. The structs/signing payloads exist ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1150), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1221)), but `submit_agent_tx` just accepts/enqueues these variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2319)), and `apply_one` only verifies system signatures while TB-13 variants return `None` for that path ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:446), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2853)). The TB-13 integration builders use all-zero signatures and are accepted ([tb_13_complete_set.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:128)). This is a Class-3 money/collateral auth blocker.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4711:Other audit results: Q2/Q3/Q7 pass on the current state-gating and share-exclusion logic. Q4 passes only for non-negative flows. Q5’s MIN invariant is mathematically right post-redeem, but it is not called by the sequencer TB-13 arms. Q9 is a challenge: the forward fence scans only fixed files and TB-13 marker spans, so a legacy import outside a marked span would bypass it ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:122)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4721:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4723:- TB13-V2 VETO: The TB-13 `AgentSignature` fields are not live-verified. The structs/signing payloads exist ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1150), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1221)), but `submit_agent_tx` just accepts/enqueues these variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2319)), and `apply_one` only verifies system signatures while TB-13 variants return `None` for that path ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:446), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2853)). The TB-13 integration builders use all-zero signatures and are accepted ([tb_13_complete_set.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:128)). This is a Class-3 money/collateral auth blocker.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4725:Other audit results: Q2/Q3/Q7 pass on the current state-gating and share-exclusion logic. Q4 passes only for non-negative flows. Q5’s MIN invariant is mathematically right post-redeem, but it is not called by the sequencer TB-13 arms. Q9 is a challenge: the forward fence scans only fixed files and TB-13 marker spans, so a legacy import outside a marked span would bypass it ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:122)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4886:src/state/typed_tx.rs:1098:/// TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3): non-negative share count.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4908:I have enough to issue the audit. I found two blocking defects: negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by the TB-13 mint/seed arms, and the TB-13 “agent-signed” variants are never live-verified at submit/apply time. I’m going to try to write the requested audit file now; this workspace is read-only, so the write may be rejected.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4915:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). A negative mint/seed credits balance, writes negative collateral, and casts the negative amount to huge `u128` shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1607), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1622), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1821), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1839)). This blocks Q1, Q6, and Q8.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4917:- TB13-V2 VETO: The TB-13 `AgentSignature` fields are not live-verified. The structs/signing payloads exist ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1150), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1221)), but `submit_agent_tx` just accepts/enqueues these variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2319)), and `apply_one` only verifies system signatures while TB-13 variants return `None` for that path ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:446), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2853)). The TB-13 integration builders use all-zero signatures and are accepted ([tb_13_complete_set.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:128)). This is a Class-3 money/collateral auth blocker.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4919:Other audit results: Q2/Q3/Q7 pass on the current state-gating and share-exclusion logic. Q4 passes only for non-negative flows. Q5’s MIN invariant is mathematically right post-redeem, but it is not called by the sequencer TB-13 arms. Q9 is a challenge: the forward fence scans only fixed files and TB-13 marker spans, so a legacy import outside a marked span would bypass it ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:122)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4929:- TB13-V1 VETO: Negative `MicroCoin` amounts are accepted by `CompleteSetMint` and `MarketSeed`. `MicroCoin` is signed and allows negative values at the type layer ([money.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/money.rs:27)); the TB-13 arms reject only `== 0`, not `< 0` ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1587), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1806)). handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6529:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_2_phase0_audit_2026-04-30.py:352:**Target**: STEP_B preflight `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md`
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6530:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_2_phase0_audit_2026-04-30.py:356:**Mandate**: STEP_B Phase-0 necessity audit; strategic / architectural / constitutional (Q1-Q8). Independent of Codex r1 (parallel, implementer-paranoid).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6531:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:52:**Q8. STEP_B-protected file violation**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6535:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:26:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetMintTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1805-1839` performs a balanced transfer. It debits `balances_t[owner]` and credits `conditional_collateral_t[event_id]` by the same `amount`. The `assert_total_ctf_conserved` invariant, extended in TB-13 to include `conditional_collateral_t` as a coin holding (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:186-189`), correctly verifies that this is a migration of funds, not a mint or burn. The integration test `sg_13_1_mint_one_coin_yields_one_yes_plus_one_no_total_coin_conserved` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:88`) explicitly confirms this.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6543:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:80:*   **(Q6.a) STEP_B_PROTOCOL violation**: **PASS**. The diff touches `src/drivers/llm_http.rs`. The restricted file list (`bus.rs`, `kernel.rs`, `wallet.rs`) does not include the `drivers/` directory. No process violation occurred.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6544:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:3:**Target**: STEP_B preflight `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md`
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6545:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:7:**Mandate**: STEP_B Phase-0 necessity audit; strategic / architectural / constitutional (Q1-Q8). Independent of Codex r1 (parallel, implementer-paranoid).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6546:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:16:The architectural framing of the STEP_B preflight is exceptionally sound. The decision to keep `dispatch_transition` pure (rejecting naive A) and the strict enforcement of the `exempt_tx_kinds` red line demonstrate a deep understanding of the TuringOS constitutional invariants. However, I am issuing a **CHALLENGE** based on Q7: the proposed 12-test acceptance battery has critical blind spots regarding `StaleParent` rejections, `submit_id` consumption on queue saturation, and the fundamental P1:8 replay invariant. These must be added to the preflight before Phase 1 begins.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6547:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:22:**Q1. STEP_B applicability** — **PASS**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6548:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:23:Relying on the C-031 "institution" catch-all is valid here. `sequencer.rs` is the absolute core of the state transition machine; it dictates what enters the canonical L4 tape and what is banished to L4.E. Changing its queue payload and error-handling paths is a fundamental architectural shift that warrants STEP_B rigor. However, relying on the catch-all is a temporary crutch; `sequencer.rs` should be formally added to `CLAUDE.md`'s restricted list (see P1).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6549:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:67:   **Concrete Remediation**: Add `src/state/sequencer.rs` to the explicit restricted-file list under the "Code Standard" section. Relying on the C-031 "institution" catch-all is valid for this audit, but explicit listing prevents future LLM agents from bypassing STEP_B due to context-window truncation of case law.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6550:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:77:Once the preflight is updated to require a 16/16 deterministic PASS (incorporating the new tests), you are cleared to proceed to STEP_B Phase 1 (branch creation and implementation).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6552:handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R3_2026-04-29.md:53:- **Line 14** (single-sentence summary, top of doc): still says "perform combined STEP_B ceremony adding a Sequencer entry-point on TuringBus + Kernel". MF4 made this single-file (TuringBus only); "combined" + "Kernel" are stale.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6553:handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:13:## Q4 STEP_B readiness
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6554:handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:17:STEP_B readiness: YES, contingent on implementing the § 2.5 fixture/ABI follow-through at the named atoms.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6555:handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:25:GO for CO P1 STEP_B / CO1.1.4 and CO1.1.5. Start the shared canonical fixtures before branch divergence, per § 2.5, but this is an implementation gate rather than another spec patch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6557:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:49:## Q4 Combined STEP_B ceremony
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6558:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:53:The proposal for a combined STEP_B ceremony is strategically sound and defensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6559:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:57:-   **`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 Interpretation**: The spec's invocation of the "minimum sufficient version" criterion is correct and binding. It applies the principle not just to the necessity of the change, but to the *atomicity* of the change being tested. This is a mature interpretation of the protocol's intent.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6560:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:79:-   **Inline Fix Appropriateness**: `CLAUDE.md` and `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` are project instructions, not the constitution. The strict "OBS not in-place" rule from the Alignment Standard applies to constitutional documents to preserve their amendment history. For operational documents, keeping them accurate and avoiding confusion for developers and auditors is paramount. Fixing the path drift inline was the correct, pragmatic choice to prevent a guaranteed, low-value CHALLENGE.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6562:handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:13:The v1.1 spec substantively addresses my prior CHALLENGE regarding incomplete pseudocode. The addition of `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`, `ReuseTx`, `finalize_reward`, and `terminal_summary` transition functions provides the necessary logical scaffolding. The explicit classification of hidden inputs (§ 2) and the promotion of environment variables to typed `QState` fields is a significant improvement in rigor and directly responds to Codex's concerns. The spec now presents a coherent, if not yet exhaustive, model for the core work lifecycle. It is sufficiently complete to serve as the binding contract for the STEP_B implementation comparison. Remaining gaps are primarily in lifecycle edge cases (see Q2/Q3), not the core transitions themselves.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6563:handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:80:## Q8. STEP_B feasibility prediction
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6564:handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:83:The prediction of surfacing 5-10 spec ambiguities during the parallel STEP_B implementation is realistic and demonstrates mature foresight. No spec of this complexity is perfect. The provided pseudocode, while much improved, still abstracts away details in functions like `materializer::apply` and `SettlementEngine::finalize`. These are the most likely sources of ambiguity.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6565:handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:85:An audit and resolution cost of 5-20 person-days is a reasonable budget for this risk. The spec is sufficiently detailed to make STEP_B a valuable and feasible process. The cost of resolving these ambiguities at the spec/implementation stage is orders of magnitude lower than fixing them post-launch. The plan is sound.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6566:handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:96:1.  **Canonical Digests:** The exact byte-level serialization format for all signed transaction types (`WorkTx`, `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`, `TerminalSummaryTx`). This must be specified to the byte to prevent signature validation mismatches between the two STEP_B implementations.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6567:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:158:Generator != Evaluator is not fully preserved for STEP_B.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6568:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:162:- STEP_B says Branch B is Codex implementation (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:65-68`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6569:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:163:- The same STEP_B lifecycle then says "Codex final review" (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:72-74`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6570:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:168:Required protocol amendment: for STEP_B atoms where Codex implements any candidate branch, final code review must be Gemini plus either Claude or a fresh Codex invocation with no branch-author context. The model that wrote the winning branch must not be the final reviewer of that branch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6571:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:175:- STEP_B atoms: ~22 x $5-10 = $110-220 (`:101`)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6572:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:188:- A heavy STEP_B implementation/review with 500k input + 50k output on GPT-5.2 Codex costs `0.500 * 1.75 + 0.050 * 14 = $1.575`. It reaches $5-10 only if repeated tool loops/failed attempts push total billed traffic above roughly 1.5M-3M mixed tokens or if a more expensive pro-class model is used.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6573:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:207:The conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS rule is good (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:81-96`), and externalizing disagreement is good (`:145-153`). But STEP_B violates Generator != Evaluator when Codex implements Branch B and then performs final review (`:65-74`, `:159-166`). Cost estimates are also not tied to actual token accounting (`:97-113`). Amend before using it on source-code atoms.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6574:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_5_2026-04-30.md:3:**Audit type**: post-development self-audit per directive § 4 Q5 narrowed Option A (Codex-only ship gate; round-4 fell back to grep-based self-verification per `c415cd2`). Replaces narrow STEP_B Phase-1c dual external audit. TB-3/TB-4 self-audit precedent extended to TB-5.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6583:  ✓ Read repo instructions and TB-13 audit artifacts
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6592:Q1/Q4/Q6/Q7 clear for non-forged, non-negative flows: mint/seed now reject `<= 0`, move balance to collateral 1:1, and `total_supply_micro` includes `conditional_collateral_t` while omitting shares ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1593), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1816), [monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:172)). Q2/Q3/RQ1 clear on state/outcome gates ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1678), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1691)). RQ3 smoke evidence shows `economic_state_reconstructed: true`, but it does not exercise TB-13 txs.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).

handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_TB_2_PHASE1C_VERDICT_2026-04-30.md:5:**Base**: `f9ace5e` (preflight v3 + charter v3 + Phase-0 audits committed to main).
handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_TB_2_PHASE1C_VERDICT_2026-04-30.md:41:| **C-1** `WORKTX_ACCEPT_DOMAIN_V1` + `worktx_canonical_hash` exported as `pub` (preflight had `pub(crate)`) | Demoted both to `pub(crate)`; promoted single composite helper `pub fn worktx_accept_state_root` for I9. Public surface shrunk 2 → 1 item. | **PASS** (R2-Q1) |
handover/audits/DUAL_AUDIT_TB_2_PHASE1C_VERDICT_2026-04-30.md:81:Substance reading: **PASS** — both r1 verdicts' actual concerns are addressed, R2-Q3 is a frame misread (the diff vs main base IS assertion-only), R2-Q4 is sandbox-blocked but locally verified, all 40 test suites green, all preflight v3 architectural constraints honored.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:52:**Q8. STEP_B-protected file violation**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:3:**Target**: STEP_B preflight `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md`
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:7:**Mandate**: STEP_B Phase-0 necessity audit; strategic / architectural / constitutional (Q1-Q8). Independent of Codex r1 (parallel, implementer-paranoid).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:16:The architectural framing of the STEP_B preflight is exceptionally sound. The decision to keep `dispatch_transition` pure (rejecting naive A) and the strict enforcement of the `exempt_tx_kinds` red line demonstrate a deep understanding of the TuringOS constitutional invariants. However, I am issuing a **CHALLENGE** based on Q7: the proposed 12-test acceptance battery has critical blind spots regarding `StaleParent` rejections, `submit_id` consumption on queue saturation, and the fundamental P1:8 replay invariant. These must be added to the preflight before Phase 1 begins.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:22:**Q1. STEP_B applicability** — **PASS**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:23:Relying on the C-031 "institution" catch-all is valid here. `sequencer.rs` is the absolute core of the state transition machine; it dictates what enters the canonical L4 tape and what is banished to L4.E. Changing its queue payload and error-handling paths is a fundamental architectural shift that warrants STEP_B rigor. However, relying on the catch-all is a temporary crutch; `sequencer.rs` should be formally added to `CLAUDE.md`'s restricted list (see P1).
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:26:The preflight's rejection of naive A is architecturally mandatory. `dispatch_transition` represents $\prod p(output | Q_t)$. It must remain a pure function. If ledger I/O were placed inside it, replaying the ledger (which is required by Art IV Boot and P1:8) would re-trigger those side effects, destroying determinism and creating a catastrophic chicken-and-egg loop. The FC2 (state mutation) / FC3 (ledger persistence) separation is absolute.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:29:The "two-L4 spines" concern is real and fatal if violated. `transition_ledger` already possesses the `LedgerEntry` schema, `LedgerWriter` trait, and `Git2LedgerWriter` backend (from CO1.7). `AcceptedLedger` was temporary RSP-0 scaffolding to prove the hash-chain math. Wiring `AcceptedLedger` into the production sequencer would create a parallel accepted ledger, violating the ChainTape single-spine contract. The preflight correctly banishes it from the production path.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:60:   **Estimated Effort**: 30 minutes to update the preflight markdown.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:67:   **Concrete Remediation**: Add `src/state/sequencer.rs` to the explicit restricted-file list under the "Code Standard" section. Relying on the C-031 "institution" catch-all is valid for this audit, but explicit listing prevents future LLM agents from bypassing STEP_B due to context-window truncation of case law.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:74:**Revise preflight.** 
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:77:Once the preflight is updated to require a 16/16 deterministic PASS (incorporating the new tests), you are cleared to proceed to STEP_B Phase 1 (branch creation and implementation).
handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:80:*   **(Q6.a) STEP_B_PROTOCOL violation**: **PASS**. The diff touches `src/drivers/llm_http.rs`. The restricted file list (`bus.rs`, `kernel.rs`, `wallet.rs`) does not include the `drivers/` directory. No process violation occurred.
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R3_2026-04-29.md:53:- **Line 14** (single-sentence summary, top of doc): still says "perform combined STEP_B ceremony adding a Sequencer entry-point on TuringBus + Kernel". MF4 made this single-file (TuringBus only); "combined" + "Kernel" are stale.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:49:## Q4 Combined STEP_B ceremony
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:53:The proposal for a combined STEP_B ceremony is strategically sound and defensible.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:57:-   **`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0 Interpretation**: The spec's invocation of the "minimum sufficient version" criterion is correct and binding. It applies the principle not just to the necessity of the change, but to the *atomicity* of the change being tested. This is a mature interpretation of the protocol's intent.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:79:-   **Inline Fix Appropriateness**: `CLAUDE.md` and `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` are project instructions, not the constitution. The strict "OBS not in-place" rule from the Alignment Standard applies to constitutional documents to preserve their amendment history. For operational documents, keeping them accurate and avoiding confusion for developers and auditors is paramount. Fixing the path drift inline was the correct, pragmatic choice to prevent a guaranteed, low-value CHALLENGE.
handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:23:- **Round 3** YOU CHALLENGE on EXIT=0 + empty PPUT_RESULT non-exhaustive case. Codex CHALLENGE on `problem_file_missing` synthetic emit + boot preflight `|| true` exit-discard. **All addressed in commit `d0d474e`**.
handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:27:1. Pre-loop adaptation-file existence preflight (Codex P0 #1): iterates ADAPTATION_IDS, checks each `$MINIF2F_DIR/MiniF2F/Test/${pid}.lean` exists, lists missing PIDs (up to 10), aborts with exit 2 if any missing. In-loop missing-file branch downgraded to race-condition guard (exit 6).
handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:28:2. Boot preflight exit-code assertion (Codex P0 #2): captures PREFLIGHT_EXIT explicitly; asserts exit != 0 AND exit != 124; then content grep for TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED. Three failure modes get distinct diagnostic messages.
handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_round4_audit.py:33:- Happy-path smoke (--smoke, 40s) all SOLVED tx=1; new preflight steps print OK
handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:119:- (Q7.d) Oracle preflight (C-012) only checks `(1:ℝ) + 1 = 2`. Is preflight depth sufficient?
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:13:## Q4 STEP_B readiness
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:17:STEP_B readiness: YES, contingent on implementing the § 2.5 fixture/ABI follow-through at the named atoms.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:25:GO for CO P1 STEP_B / CO1.1.4 and CO1.1.5. Start the shared canonical fixtures before branch divergence, per § 2.5, but this is an implementation gate rather than another spec patch.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:158:Generator != Evaluator is not fully preserved for STEP_B.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:162:- STEP_B says Branch B is Codex implementation (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:65-68`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:163:- The same STEP_B lifecycle then says "Codex final review" (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:72-74`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:168:Required protocol amendment: for STEP_B atoms where Codex implements any candidate branch, final code review must be Gemini plus either Claude or a fresh Codex invocation with no branch-author context. The model that wrote the winning branch must not be the final reviewer of that branch.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:175:- STEP_B atoms: ~22 x $5-10 = $110-220 (`:101`)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:188:- A heavy STEP_B implementation/review with 500k input + 50k output on GPT-5.2 Codex costs `0.500 * 1.75 + 0.050 * 14 = $1.575`. It reaches $5-10 only if repeated tool loops/failed attempts push total billed traffic above roughly 1.5M-3M mixed tokens or if a more expensive pro-class model is used.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:207:The conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS rule is good (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/TRI_MODEL_ORCHESTRATION_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26.md:81-96`), and externalizing disagreement is good (`:145-153`). But STEP_B violates Generator != Evaluator when Codex implements Branch B and then performs final review (`:65-74`, `:159-166`). Cost estimates are also not tied to actual token accounting (`:97-113`). Amend before using it on source-code atoms.
handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_12_ship_audit.py:56:6. Does the flat NodePositionsIndex extend cleanly to TB-13 CompleteSet (which will introduce real YES/NO claims) without schema collision?
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:13:The v1.1 spec substantively addresses my prior CHALLENGE regarding incomplete pseudocode. The addition of `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`, `ReuseTx`, `finalize_reward`, and `terminal_summary` transition functions provides the necessary logical scaffolding. The explicit classification of hidden inputs (§ 2) and the promotion of environment variables to typed `QState` fields is a significant improvement in rigor and directly responds to Codex's concerns. The spec now presents a coherent, if not yet exhaustive, model for the core work lifecycle. It is sufficiently complete to serve as the binding contract for the STEP_B implementation comparison. Remaining gaps are primarily in lifecycle edge cases (see Q2/Q3), not the core transitions themselves.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:80:## Q8. STEP_B feasibility prediction
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:83:The prediction of surfacing 5-10 spec ambiguities during the parallel STEP_B implementation is realistic and demonstrates mature foresight. No spec of this complexity is perfect. The provided pseudocode, while much improved, still abstracts away details in functions like `materializer::apply` and `SettlementEngine::finalize`. These are the most likely sources of ambiguity.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:85:An audit and resolution cost of 5-20 person-days is a reasonable budget for this risk. The spec is sufficiently detailed to make STEP_B a valuable and feasible process. The cost of resolving these ambiguities at the spec/implementation stage is orders of magnitude lower than fixing them post-launch. The plan is sound.
handover/audits/GEMINI_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:96:1.  **Canonical Digests:** The exact byte-level serialization format for all signed transaction types (`WorkTx`, `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`, `TerminalSummaryTx`). This must be specified to the byte to prevent signature validation mismatches between the two STEP_B implementations.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_round3_audit.sh:21:- **Round 3 (this audit)**: determine whether the round-2 fix resolves your previous VETO AND whether it introduces NEW defects. Specifically scrutinize the new crash-vs-timeout discrimination logic and the boot preflight.
handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_round3_audit.sh:27:2. Pre-batch evaluator boot preflight: invokes `evaluator /nonexistent.lean`; if stderr contains TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED, runner exits 2 at preflight stage — zero API spend wasted.
